MBGL.valuation_basis: POST-SPIN SoP for Mobility Global (CARFAX + automotiveMastermind + Mobility insights), spun from S&P Global. Single high-quality vertical info-services asset: FY2025 revenue $1,750M, Adjusted EBITDA $711M (41% margin, ~81% subscription) per Form 10-12B/A EX-99.1 filed Jun 2026 (SEC EDGAR, CIK 0002090312). EV = $711M x 17.0x = $12,087M. 17x comp: premium subscription-data peer set — TransUnion ~14x, Verisk ~25x, parent SPGI ~20x EV/EBITDA; CARFAX's near-monopoly VIN-history data + 81% recurring rev supports a mid-to-high-teens multiple. Net debt: $2,000M new senior notes ($650M@5.05% '29 + $650M@5.45% '31 + $700M@6.05% '36) less ~$200M retained cash = $1,800M (Form 10, pro-forma Mar-31-2026 cap table; $1,900M net cash dividend up to parent). Shares 295.077M (1:1 distribution on 295,077,160 SPGI shares as of May-15-2026). FV=(12,087-1,800-0)/295.1=$34.86. reference_price $30.00 = ESTIMATED when-issued first-trade (MBGL not yet trading; record Jun-15, WI ~Jun-26, distribution/NYSE Jul-1-2026); modest discount to fair for a newly-levered sub-scale orphan. reference_rr 0.68 at that estimate. Floor = de-rated no-deal standalone at 12x (levered sub-scale info orphan, forced-seller index pressure): (711x12-1800)/295.1=$22.81.

sop_components.basis: Subscription-data comps: TransUnion ~14x, Verisk ~25x, SPGI ~20x EV/EBITDA; 81% recurring + VIN-data moat -> 17x (Form 10-12B/A EX-99.1, Jun 2026)

NSC.valuation_basis: Merger-arb, stock+cash. UP/NS definitive agreement (announced Jul 29 2025): each NSC share = 1.0 UNP share + $88.82 cash. deal_price = 1.0 x UNP $269.785 (live 2026-06-08) + $88.82 = $358.61. Both shareholder votes passed Nov 14 2025 (~99%). Deal is now gated solely on STB: STB accepted the REVISED application May 28 2026 (PR-26-13) but held proceedings IN ABEYANCE, froze the environmental review, and ordered supplemental info by Jul 27 2026 (issues: competition, shipper access, service assurance) -> genuine regulatory risk, realistic close pushes into 2027. Undisturbed $254 (Jul 16 2025, last close before bid speculation; 30-day VWAP ~$256 implied by $320/1.25). At live $310.78 the ~$48 gross spread implies the market is pricing a high break/delay probability.

VERA.valuation_basis: BARBELL, not a ratio. Two hard binary catalysts for atacicept in IgAN: (1) Jul 7 2026 PDUFA accelerated-approval decision (priority review granted), (2) ORIGIN-3 confirmatory eGFR readout pulled forward to Q3 2026 (FDA-aligned). De-risking: ORIGIN-3 proteinuria endpoint ALREADY met decisively at the prespecified interim (46% reduction from baseline, 42% vs placebo, p<0.0001 at wk36; published NEJM Nov 6 2025). win_prob ~0.85 calibrated off (a) endpoint already hit, (b) priority review, (c) regulatory precedent of FDA approving IgAN agents on the urine-protein surrogate (sparsentan/Filspari, Tarpeyo, iptacopan/Fabhalta all cleared on proteinuria). target_on_win $66 = re-rate to BofA peak-IgAN-sales-based PO (~$2.2B nominal peak); bull-case sell-side spans $66-100. downside_on_loss ~$11 = balance-sheet floor ($779M pro forma cash / ~71.8M sh ~= $10.85). At live $32.27: win EV +104%, loss EV -66%; prob-weighted expected return ~ +79%.
VERA.prob_basis: Endpoint already met (ORIGIN-3 proteinuria 42% vs placebo p<0.0001, NEJM Nov 6 2025) + FDA priority review + IgAN-class precedent of accelerated approval on the proteinuria surrogate (Filspari/Tarpeyo/Fabhalta)

HONA.valuation_basis: POST-SPIN SoP for Honeywell Aerospace (final leg of HON 3-way split; segments Electronic Solutions + Engines & Power Systems + Control Systems). FY2025 net sales $17,404M, total segment profit (segment Adj EBIT) $4,085M; EBITDA ~$4,450M (EBIT $4,085M + depreciation $275M + ~$90M other amort) per Form 10-12B/A EX-99.1 filed 2026 (SEC EDGAR, CIK 0002089271). EV = $4,450M x 15.0x = $66,750M. 15x comp: tier-1 A&D supplier peer set GE Aerospace ~22x, TransDigm ~20x, HEICO ~28x, RTX ~14x; HONA gets a BELOW-pure-play multiple (broader OE-mix, more cyclical, ~23% segment margin vs aftermarket-pure peers) -> 15x. Net debt = $16,000M senior notes (issued Mar-16-2026) less ~$1,000M retained cash = $15,000M. Adjustments (SIGNED) = +$1,125M trademark-license liability ('Honeywell' name, 60 x $18.75M monthly, <5yr) MINUS $2,727M net defined-benefit PENSION ASSET contributed to SpinCo = -$1,602M net (a net positive to equity). Shares 316.827M (1 HONA per 2 HON shares; 316,826,559 per Form 10 pro-forma). FV=(66,750-15,000-(-1,602))/316.8=$168.41. CORRECTION confirmed: funded debt is $16.0B (not the garbled $24B). reference_price $150.00 = ESTIMATED when-issued first-trade (HONA not yet trading; record Jun-15, distribution Jun-29-2026, NASDAQ); large clean A&D spin draws index/passive buying so a smaller discount to fair than MBGL. reference_rr 0.49. Floor = de-rated standalone at 11x (cyclical-OE roll, aero-cycle fear): (4450x11-15000+(1125-2727))/316.8=$112.22.

sop_components.basis: A&D suppliers GE Aero ~22x / TDG ~20x / HEICO ~28x / RTX ~14x; below pure-play for broader OE mix + cyclicality -> 15x (Form 10-12B/A EX-99.1)

MGNI.valuation_basis: AdX-divestiture beneficiary, treated as a regulatory BINARY (overriding 'forced_seller' lane: MGNI is a beneficiary of a structural-share gift, not a compelled seller). Google ~60% SSP share, Magnite ~6%, PubMatic ~4% (ppc.land/StockStory 2026). Brinkema found liability Apr'25; remedies closing args Nov 21 2025; NO ruling yet as of Jun 8 2026 (Digiday/AdExchanger), judge has signaled skepticism on structural relief. WIN = AdX ordered divested + barred 10yrs: independent SSPs capture the freed 60% share at ~85-90% incremental margin (Magnite guided 90% incr; Crossroads Capital) -> EBITDA roughly doubles off ~$620M contribution-ex-TAC, re-rate to ~$27 (prior 52wk high $26.65 set on remedy optimism). LOSE = behavioral-only / multi-year appeal: stays a 13x-EBITDA SSP, de-rates to ~$11 (near 52wk low $10.82). FY25 rev $713.95M, Adj EBITDA ~$156M (EV/EBITDA 13.1x), net CASH ~$275M (ND/EBITDA -1.76); 142.6M sh; ref $14.61. Barbell EV at $14.61 (p=0.30) = +8.1%: 0.30*(+84.8%) + 0.70*(-24.7%); reward:risk 3.4:1. Net-cash + 13x standalone caps downside vs option-like upside; better than PUBM (still below 200d $16.0, 6% vs 4% share to compound onto).
MGNI.prob_basis: ~30% structural-divestiture-that-survives-appeal probability: Brinkema repeatedly questioned structural remedies and timing ('window dressing' hearings, The Current/AdExchanger Sep-Nov'25), leaning behavioral; even a divest order faces years of appeals before share actually flows. 30% = calibrated joint prob of (order is structural) x (survives/implements) within horizon.

PUBM.valuation_basis: Same AdX-divestiture BINARY as MGNI (override 'forced_seller'->binary_prob; beneficiary not seller), highest beta to a forced break-up. PubMatic ~4% SSP share, smallest base => highest operating leverage on captured share, open-web concentrated. WIN = AdX divested: re-rate to ~$19 (open-web volume + ~80%+ incremental margins on share gains off ~$1.14B rev). LOSE = behavioral-only/appeal: the antitrust premium ($6.15->$11.27, +83% off the 52wk low) unwinds, stock gives back to ~$7.5. Rosenblatt initiated Buy explicitly on 'antitrust upside' (Investing.com), confirming how much of today's price is the catalyst bet. TTM rev ~$1.14B, Q1'26 net CASH $102M, 47.1M sh, mkt cap $527M, ~0.4x EV/sales; ref $11.27; 52wk 6.15-13.88. ENTRY-SENSITIVE / SIZE SMALL: barbell EV at $11.27 (p=0.30) = -2.9%: 0.30*(+68.5%) + 0.70*(-33.5%) -> NEGATIVE expected value at the current near-52wk-high price because the stock has already discounted much of the catalyst. Reward:risk 2.05:1, inferior to MGNI's 3.4:1. Turns positive-EV only on a pullback toward ~$9 (priceAvg50 $9.65) or if structural-order conviction rises above ~0.35. Right catalyst, wrong entry today.
PUBM.prob_basis: Same ~30% structural-and-survives probability as MGNI (single shared catalyst: Brinkema AdX remedy). No name-specific edge on the legal outcome; PUBM differs only in payoff geometry, not in odds.

PRX.AS.valuation_basis: DEMOTED. The Delivery-Hero/forced-seller framing was the wrong read: the DHER-overhang-clearing catalyst re-rates the UNDERLYING (DHER), NOT Prosus the compelled seller, so there is no event-driven re-rate that accrues to PRX from selling DHER (it sold 4.5% to Uber for ~EUR270m Apr'26 - a rounding error vs the holdco). PRX is therefore valued as a Tencent-proxy holdco on NAV-discount mechanics, not a special-situation. Live EUR39.52 sits at a ~35% discount to NAV; NAV/sh ~EUR60.8 (Tencent = 76% of GAV, ~22.8% of Tencent / 2,066m shrs; equities123/Prosus IR 2026). The only real 'catalyst' is the self-funded buyback narrowing the discount (54% Tencent-funded since Jan'26). Not a clean special-situation comp -> floor WIDENED to the 52wk low EUR37.37 (Tencent-beta downside; PRX NAV moves ~$1.35B per 1% Tencent). fair_value EUR46.21 assumes discount narrows 35%->24% on continued buyback, consistent with Morgan Stanley OW ('29% upside, discount peaks'). R:R 3.1:1 (EUR6.69 up vs EUR2.16 down) but this is a Tencent/China-beta carry trade, NOT the event-driven asymmetry the book hunts; correctly demoted out of the catalyst sleeve.

SPCX.valuation_basis: CONTINGENT index-flow barbell. SpaceX roadshow fixed IPO price $135 (CNBC 2026-06-03), ~555.6M shares sold, ~$1.77T cap, list Jun-12. NDX fast-entry ~15 trading days post-IPO (~Jul-6) forces ~$7B+ single-day NDX/QQQ buying into a 3-5% float (~$45-100B tradable) = mechanical demand 15-30% of float in a compressed window, gamma-squeeze setup (SpotGamma 2026-06-04; Morningstar: 'price will likely survive separation and may ascend'). S&P leg CONFIRMED OFF (S&P DJI reaffirmed rules Jun-5, $4.94B 2025 GAAP loss). Single kill-risk = IPO slip/pull. WIN=IPO prices on schedule + NDX inclusion squeeze -> ~$165 (+22% vs $135). LOSS=IPO slips/pulled, no catalyst; Morningstar fair value <half the $1.75T target -> reprices ~$115. win_prob 0.80 (roadshow-stage fixed-price IPO rarely pulled; fast-entry rule is mechanical).
SPCX.prob_basis: Roadshow-stage IPO with fixed $135 price and full bank syndicate historically completes ~80%+; NDX fast-entry inclusion is rule-mechanical once listed (Nasdaq 15-day fast-entry change, Motley Fool/Morningstar 2026-06-06). Only material kill-risk is an IPO date slip.

VERI.valuation_basis: Instrument = 1.75% Convertible Senior Notes due Nov-15-2026, $45.6M par outstanding (10-K/10-Q, $45.392M net of discount on B/S 2026-03-31). Liquidity: $15.087M cash + $26.717M AR vs $45.6M par; $45.8M working-cap deficit; going concern; refi only 'in discussions,' nothing committed (10-Q 2026-05-14). RECOVERY ANCHOR: Nov-2025 the co. repurchased $45.67M par converts in privately-negotiated TDR for $39.049M cash + 625k shares = ~85.5c on the dollar (most recent observable mark). Refi/exchange likely clears remaining converts ~80-85c. recovery_value=82 (cents per $100 par). downside_floor=45c: coercive equitization / Ch.11 with thin cash-vs-par and negative working capital. NO clean trading comp for the bond -> floor widened. reference_price=70 (est. current distressed convert mark, cents); reference_rr=(82-70)/(70-45)=0.48. Equity $1.715 is the dilutive residual, NOT the instrument.
VERI.instrument_ref: 1.75% Convertible Senior Notes due Nov-15-2026 ($45.6M par)

AHT.valuation_basis: Instrument = suspended preferred (Series D-M), $25.00/sh liquidation preference, dividends suspended & ACCRUING since Jan-2026 (PR 2026-01-13). Asset coverage: Stanger valuation (Dec-31-2025) found company equity value EXCEEDED total preferred liquidation preference for ALL series -> preferred is asset-covered, common has residual. Catalyst = Highland non-recourse loan ($723.6M, ~65% of appraised value, FINAL maturity Jul-9-2026) refi + strategic resolution -> arrears recognized, dividends resume. Reference instrument = AHT-PD (most liquid pref) at $4.445. RECOVERY: a covered pref that resumes paying in a distressed REIT resolution re-rates to ~50% of par incl. accrued arrears -> recovery_value=$12.50. downside_floor=$3.00: Highland default/Ch.11 impairs pref toward common-equity level. NO clean comp (idiosyncratic distressed REIT cap stack) -> floor kept conservative/wide. reference_rr=(12.50-4.445)/(4.445-3.00)=5.57 (deep-distressed optionality).
AHT.instrument_ref: Series D-M cumulative preferred ($25.00 liq pref); ref = AHT-PD

SNBR.valuation_basis: Instrument traded = common equity at $0.4731 (lender-mandated distressed sale 'in full', Guggenheim engaged). Cap stack: $25M bridge term loan SOFR+8% maturing Jun-30-2026; $30M min-liquidity covenant waived only until first week after Jul-1; forbearance from existing lenders; ~$588M revolver; preparing for possible Ch.11 (8-K 2026-04-28; reports Jun-4). Stock +66% TODAY on a 228M-share SHORT-SQUEEZE (retraced from prior ~135% spike). RECOVERY to common in a lender-mandated sale of an over-levered specialty-bedding retailer is de minimis: value flows to the revolver/bridge lenders. recovery_value=$0.10 (thin residual equity stub in sale/restructuring). downside_floor=$0.02: Ch.11 equity ~= 0. This is a SHORT/FADE: target ($0.10) is BELOW reference ($0.4731) -> reference_rr=(0.10-0.4731)/(0.4731-0.02)=-0.82, i.e. the squeeze is overpriced vs recovery, downside dominates.
SNBR.instrument_ref: Common equity (residual to $25M bridge SOFR+8% Jun-30-2026 + ~$588M revolver)

GPC.valuation_basis: SoP for Genuine Parts split into Automotive (NAPA) + Industrial (Motion). FY2025 segment EBITDA per 10-K (SEC EDGAR CIK 0000040987, gpc-20251231): North America Automotive $672.2M (7.1% mgn, sales ~$9.47B) + International Automotive $544.2M (9.3% mgn, sales ~$5.85B) = Automotive $1,216.4M; Industrial/Motion $1,146.4M (12.9% mgn, sales ~$8.89B). Automotive EV = $1,216.4M x 8.0x = $9,731M (2-step auto-parts distribution comp ~7-8x; AAP weak, GPC mid-quality). Industrial EV = $1,146.4M x 10.0x = $11,464M (industrial-distribution comp: Applied Industrial AIT ~14x, WESCO ~9x -> 10x for Motion). Sum EV $21,195M. Net debt $7,798M (incl ~$1,739M cap leases, FY2025 BS). Adjustments +$400M for stranded/dis-synergy corporate (FY25 corporate EBITDA drag -1.5% of sales). Shares 138.9M. FV=(21,195-7,798-400)/138.9=$93.57. Live ref $97.05 -> NO_UPSIDE: stock already sold off (52wk high $151) on the soft-spin announcement, pricing in much of the value; reference_rr negative (-0.09). NOT ACTIVE: no Form 10 filed -> no dated forced-flow catalyst. Floor = de-rated NO-DEAL standalone at 7x blended on combined $2,362.8M EBITDA (low-growth distributor, ~2% organic): (2362.8x7-7798-400)/138.9=$60.05 -- ~38% below live and BELOW the $90.78 52wk low (chart low embeds spin hope).

sop_components.basis: 2-step auto-parts distribution 7-8x EV/EBITDA (AAP/peer); 10-K FY2025 segment EBITDA $672.2M+$544.2M
Industrial distribution: AIT ~14x / WESCO ~9x -> 10x; 10-K FY2025 segment EBITDA $1,146.4M (12.9% mgn)

JNJ.valuation_basis: NON-ACTIONABLE soft-spin. J&J to separate Orthopaedics (DePuy Synthes, ~$9.2B 2024 sales, ~$6.82B 9M-2025 -0.3% YoY, declining laggard) from MedTech; announced Oct-14-2025, 18-24mo window (~2027), NO Form 10, no forced-flow event. EDGE IS THIN: $558B-cap, exhaustively covered, and DePuy is ~10% of MedTech / ~3% of a $94.2B revenue base -> immaterial to the parent. FAIR VALUE set at market-supported ~$232: JNJ trades on P/E (~21x FY2025 EPS $11.03 = $232), NOT EV/EBITDA -- a mechanical EBITDA-SoP (IM ~$27B EBITDA x13x + MedTech-ex-ortho ~$6.6B x17x + DePuy ~$1.9B x9x, net debt $28.2B, 2,428.6M sh) prints only ~$186, which materially UNDER-reads pharma quality/pipeline and would be misleading; I therefore do NOT emit fabricated sop_components and anchor to the P/E-supported level. reference_price live $232.13; reference_rr ~0 (no special-situation upside from this catalyst). Floor $192.00 = de-rated standalone at a trough ~17.5x P/E (pharma-cliff/talc-litigation fear). Reads NO_UPSIDE / no edge -- honest.

ETN.valuation_basis: SoP for Eaton; Mobility Group (Vehicle + eMobility, ~$3.1B sales / ~13% margin) to spin by end-Q1-2027 (announced Jan-26-2026; Form 10 NOT yet filed). FY2025 segment EBITDA est from 10-K segment data (CIK 0001551182): Electrical Americas $4,250M (rev $13,276M) x 24.0x=$102,000M [data-center-driven premium, peers 22-26x]; Electrical Global $1,640M (rev $6,815M) x 20.0x=$32,800M; Aerospace $1,060M (rev $4,249M) x 22.0x=$23,320M [A&D 20-24x]; Vehicle $480M (rev $2,505M, FY24 op margin 18%) x 8.0x=$3,840M [driveline DAN/BWA 6-8x]; eMobility $30M (rev $618M, ~breakeven) x 12.0x=$360M. Sum EV $162,320M. Net debt $10,547M (FY2025 BS). Shares 388.2M. FV=(162,320-10,547)/388.2=$390.97. Live ref $403.31 -> NO_UPSIDE: the Mobility orphan is only ~$4.2B EV of a ~$162B SoP -- value-neutral/slightly negative at a price already near the 52wk high ($435); reference_rr -0.15. The spin does not create a special-situation edge on a $156B-cap parent. Floor = de-rated standalone at 18x on sum-segment EBITDA ~$7,480M if the data-center premium (currently ~26x) compresses: (7480x18-10547)/388.2=$319.66.

sop_components.basis: Data-center electrical premium; peers 22-26x EV/EBITDA
Electrical equipment 18-22x
A&D supplier 20-24x
Driveline/components DAN/BWA 6-8x; orphan low-growth ~2%
Sub-scale, ~breakeven; optionality multiple

GOCO.valuation_basis: Thesis HARDENED past the WATCH framing: on 7-Jun-2026 GoHealth FILED a prepackaged Chapter 11 (Delaware) with 100% lender support, >60% Class A holders, >99% Holdings unitholders (8-K 2026-06-08; explains the -44.9% crash to $0.37 today). THE EQUITY IS NOT THE PLAY: eligible Class A common + Holdings units split a $10.0M cash equity recovery pool; all other equity canceled (Series A pref reinstated). Recovery to common = $10.0M / ~28.85M units (16.23M Class A + 12.62M Class B, FMP/10-Q 2026-03-31) = ~$0.347/sh, BELOW spot $0.3715, and Class A delists to OTC -> reference_rr negative; the stub is overpriced vs its own pool. THE PLAY IS THE TERM LOAN: the ~$701.8M Norvax term loan (eff. ~14.7%) equitizes -> lenders take ownership of reorganized GoHealth (change of control). instrument_ref = that TL. Floor on the common ~ residual pool less timeline/fee leakage ~$0.10. valuation_asof 2026-06-08; sources: GoHealth 8-K 2026-06-07/08 + 10-Q 2026-03-31 ($39.9M cash vs $701.8M term loans, going-concern).
GOCO.instrument_ref: GoHealth/Norvax senior secured term loan ($701.8M, ~14.7% eff.) - equitized into ~100% of reorganized equity; the listed Class A common only shares a $10.0M cash pool (~$0.35/sh, below spot)

QVC.valuation_basis: ACTIVE in-court prepack (filed 16-Apr-2026 SDTX; combined disclosure/confirmation 4-5,8-9 Jun; target emergence/effective ~8-Jun-2026 = today). Old QVCGA/QVCGP/QVCC WIPED (no recovery). THE INSTRUMENT IS THE FULCRUM: QVC, Inc. senior secured notes (~$2.15B) + RCF/credit facility (~$2.9B) equitize >$5B funded debt into ~100% of Reorganized QVC equity + cash + $1.275B takeback debt; total funded debt cut ~$6.6B -> ~$1.3B. Evercore implies New QVC equity value $998-1,398M (mid $1,198M, NET of Mitsui 40% minority) on $2,050M net EV less $1,275M takeback + $423M emergence cash; FY26E OIBDA $602M -> ~2.1-2.2x gross / ~1.2x net leverage. Base-case secured recovery 70-90% (mid ~80c on the dollar) delivered as cash+takeback+equity; the RARITY is the plan's commitment to PURSUE an NYSE/Nasdaq listing for the new equity (rare for a reorg stub -> liquidity/re-rate kicker). Notes traded ~45c pre-confirmation -> target ~80c, downside ~40c if timeline slips (but RSA-backed prepack = limited break risk). reference_price/recovery expressed in cents per $100 face of the fulcrum notes. valuation_asof 2026-06-08; sources: QVC 8-K 2026-04-17 + ex99-2 (Evercore), Davis Polk RSA note, Kroll case site, fulcrum-note analysis (nickclayton substack ~45c).
QVC.instrument_ref: QVC, Inc. senior secured notes (fulcrum) + revolving credit facility -> ~100% of Reorganized QVC equity (Evercore mid equity $1,198M, net of 40% Mitsui) + $1.275B takeback debt + cash; old QVCGA/QVCGP/QVCC common+pref wiped

NFE.valuation_basis: ACTIVE LME (UK Restructuring Plan + Ch15; RSA signed 17-Mar-2026, 95-97%+ of ~$5.8B corp debt; terms in DEFR14A). Sequence: vote deadline 9-Jun -> annual meeting/vote 17-Jun -> plan meetings 15-Jun -> targeted close Q3-2026. Structure: NFE splits into creditor-owned private BrazilCo + publicly-traded 'New NFE' (LNG-to-power). New NFE corp debt cut ~$5.7B -> ~$527.5M new term loans; creditors also get up to $2.5B convertible preferred (3yr, PIK 3%/5%/7%) + 65% of New NFE common; EXISTING common falls to 35% at close. THE KILLER OVERHANG: the $2.5B pref MANDATORILY CONVERTS after 3yrs into 87% of fully-diluted equity -> on look-through, legacy common = 35% x 13% residual = ~4.55% of FD. Value: New NFE 2027E adj EBITDA ~$415M+ x ~8x = ~$3.32B EV - $527.5M term loans = ~$2.79B common pool; existing holders' 4.55% = ~$127M / 285.63M sh = ~$0.44/sh look-through. Sensitized 8-9x and $415-500M EBITDA -> ~$0.44-0.63; set fair_value ~$0.50. At spot $0.5326 the common already prices ABOVE look-through -> reference_rr negative; the stub is deep-OTM option value on the LNG ramp (2027 EBITDA to $415M+), near-wipe if plan executes as written (SA: 'limited option value for common'). Floor ~$0.30 on plan-slip/going-concern (10-Q flags substantial doubt; multiple facilities in payment default). instrument_ref = New NFE common stub. valuation_asof 2026-06-08; sources: NFE 8-K/press 17-Mar-2026 (nfepressrelease), DEFR14A/DEF14A 2026, 10-Q Q1-2026 (cash $189.9M, total debt ~$8.3B incl project debt, stockholders' deficit).
NFE.instrument_ref: New NFE common stock (existing-holder stub) - 35% at close, diluted to ~4.55% of fully-diluted after the $2.5B convertible preferred mandatorily converts to 87% of FD equity in yr 3; New NFE term loans ~$527.5M senior

SAKS.valuation_basis: Ch11 plan CONFIRMED Jun-5-2026 (Judge Perez, S.D. Tex.); emergence ~Jun-22. Instrument = the only public claim, 11.000% Sr Secured Notes due 2029 (~$2.2B face from the Aug-2025 exchange; ~92% tendered). RECOVERY BRIDGE per $100 face: post-emergence cap structure is ~$1.2B debt ($750M term loan + $347M ABL) + ~$500M court-approved exit financing, backed by real-estate NAV appraised at $4.4B (S&P) to $5-6B (flagship + owned/ground-leased stores + IP, ~13M sq ft). Asset coverage hugely exceeds the ~$1.2-1.7B reinstated debt, so the senior SPV/ABL tranche is near-par; the impairment sits in the 2nd-out/3rd-out 11% notes + new-equity stub the old noteholders receive. Blended post-emergence package value ~$62 per $100 face (senior tranches ~par, junior+equity discounted for illiquidity/holdco-equity-only collateral and execution risk to the FY30 $9B GMV / double-digit EBITDA plan). Bonds-only, illiquid, marked ~$52 into confirmation. Source: prnewswire 302793045 (Jun-5); retaildive.com/news/saks-global-bankruptcy-exit-june (exit debt); octus.com (collateral = upstreamed equity value, not direct lien); S&P/Moody's downgrades to CC/Caa3.
SAKS.instrument_ref: 11.000% Senior Secured Notes due 2029 (Saks Global Enterprises)
SAKS.prob_basis: Plan already confirmed (not probabilistic on the event); residual recovery uncertainty is on real-estate monetization and turnaround execution, hence floor widened to ~45 (liquidation/illiquid-mark) given bonds-only and equity-only (not direct-lien) collateral on the junior tranches.

SATS.valuation_basis: RE-FRAMED from 'distressed default' to ASSET-MONETIZATION de-lever: the Jun-1 skip of ~$183M DISH DBS coupons (30-day grace -> ~Jul-1) is deliberate liquidity-preservation, NOT insolvency. EchoStar is bridging to ~$20.25B NET proceeds from the AT&T spectrum sale (~$23B gross, ~30MHz 3.45GHz + ~20MHz 600MHz; DOJ+FCC approved, FCC order pending-final), expected to close mid-2026. Reporting (PitchBook) indicates intent to make the missed May/June coupons and skip only Jul-1, i.e. a cure-on-cash, consistent with the 2025 skip-then-cure base rate. CAPITAL-RETURN MODEL: the $20.25B inflow (~$70/sh gross on ~290M sh) repairs the balance sheet (DBS notes covered, leverage slashed); residual_value = the remaining franchise (Boost retail wireless, Hughes, EchoStar Mobile + retained nationwide spectrum, ~2% SpaceX stake). Equity $114.31 live; analyst targets cluster ~$129 (high $155 TD Cowen May-18, consensus $109-147). fair_value ~$135 on deal-close + de-lever; floor ~$90 = mid-grace stress level (well above the $14.90 52wk-low, which predates the AT&T deal). Source: SEC 8-K sats-20260601x8k; rcrwireless/cnbc (Aug-2025 $23B deal); marketbeat/public.com targets.
SATS.prob_basis: Not a clean binary. Base rate = quiet cure (ran the identical skip-then-cure twice in 2025) + deal already DOJ/FCC-approved, so break risk is low but timing/FCC-final and can-kick optionality cap the re-rate. R:R<1 at $114 because much of the deal value is in the tape; floor anchored to grace-period stress not cash-liquidation.

TPTA.valuation_basis: Cleanest public LME instrument. 6.00% Senior Notes due Jun-30-2026 (~$54.5M face, $25 par baby bond), live $22.30. Registered EXCHANGE OFFER (commenced May-7, expiry EXTENDED to Jun-10-2026): each $25 of old notes -> $20.00 new 8.00% Sr SECURED Notes due Dec-31-2028 (pari passu, shares existing-secured collateral) + $5.00 CASH. RECOVERY per $25 face: $5 hard cash + new 8% secured 2.5yr note. New note fair value ~$18-19 (8% coupon and senior-secured/collateralized, but issuer carries going-concern doubt: 10-Q ~$5M cash vs ~$69.6M debt due) -> total package ~$23-24. fair_value ~$24. Downside if exchange fails: maturity wall hits Jun-30 with insufficient cash -> default, but SECURED status preserves substantial recovery; orderly floor ~$15-16 (year-low $8.75 = panic, not base case given collateral). Source: GlobeNewswire 3290735 (commencement); S-4/A tm2612950; stocktitan 425 filings ($20+$5 terms, extension).
TPTA.prob_basis: Exchange consent already broadly secured (extension is mop-up), so deal-completion prob high; but at $22.30 the $24 package is largely priced -> thin upside. Floor at ~$15 reflects secured-claim recovery in a failed-exchange hard default, not the $8.75 distress low.

INIO.valuation_basis: Day-2 IPO (priced $27 6/4/26, secondary by AI Alpine/Advent+ADIA). FY25 rev $2.637B (+22%), adj EBITDA $549M; order intake >$6B annualized + $2.3GW VoltaGrid vs $2.64B rev base; DC orders $27M(2023)->$2.28B(2025)->$1.01B Q1'26 alone. BINARY barbell. WIN: backlog converts, recip-engine scarcity premium holds -> ~32x EV/EBITDA on FY27E ~$900M EBITDA = $28.8B EV less ~$1B net debt = $27.8B/750M sh ~ $37. LOSS: AI-power capex digestion, orders normalize -> de-rate to diversified power-gen/industrial ~16x on FY26E ~$650M EBITDA = $10.4B EV less $1B = $9.4B/750M ~ $12.5 (IPO unwind). win_prob 0.55 (contracted backlog) but valuation extreme (live ~46x trailing). EV% = .55x(37-33.72)/33.72 + .45x(12.5-33.72)/33.72 = -0.23 -> priced for perfection on day 2. Src: Manufacturing Dive/Yahoo 6/3/26, Renaissance/Investing.com 6/4/26.
INIO.prob_basis: Order intake >$6B annualized vs $2.64B rev + $2.3GW VoltaGrid contracted => conversion more likely than not, but day-2 valuation (~46x trailing EBITDA) leaves no margin; calibrated 0.55.

FCX.valuation_basis: Copper deficit (>400kt 2026) + Grasberg (world #2 mine) force majeure since Sep-2025; phased restart H1'26, ~35% below pre-incident in 2026, pre-incident rates only in 2027. 1,437.6M sh, live $63.885 (52wk $35.15-$72.09; already ~partly priced). BINARY on copper deck. WIN: deficit + slow Grasberg lift realized copper toward ~$5.50/lb cycle peak -> re-rate to street high ~$78. LOSS: copper mean-reverts to ~$4.00/lb (Grasberg restarts faster + demand softens) -> de-rated standalone ~$48 (NOT the $35 52wk low, which embedded pre-deficit $3.50 copper). win_prob 0.50 (deficit real but much priced; Grasberg timing two-sided). EV% = .50x(78-63.885)/63.885 + .50x(48-63.885)/63.885 = -0.014 -> ~fairly priced. Src: Benchmark Minerals (2026 deficit), FCX 4Q25 8-K, thedeepdive/Investing.com (Grasberg FM).
FCX.prob_basis: Copper deficit confirmed by Benchmark + Grasberg FM is real, but COMEX already spiked to ~$4.84 and FCX sits ~$64 vs $35 low => much priced; Grasberg restart pace is the swing factor. Calibrated 0.50.

MP.valuation_basis: Only scaled US mine-to-magnet; DoD 10yr $110/kg NdPr floor + 100% offtake + Apple $500M. CORE THESIS ALREADY FIRED (+226% on DoD deal); live $57.575 (52wk $23.26-$100.25), 178.0M sh. Remaining move = execution. BINARY. WIN: 10kt magnet capacity + 500t/mo NdPr run-rate by end-2026 + DoD floor -> street target $82 (DA Davidson/Canaccord, Jun-2026). LOSS: ramp slips, RE prices fade as China re-floods, DoD-floor premium compresses -> de-rated standalone ~$35 (strips most of the +226% pop; above pre-deal ~$23 base but well below live). win_prob 0.45 (rich, mostly priced, execution-heavy). EV% = .45x(82-57.575)/57.575 + .55x(35-57.575)/57.575 = -0.025 -> mostly priced. Src: DA Davidson PT $82 (6/5/26), Canaccord $82, Seeking Alpha 500t/mo run-rate.
MP.prob_basis: Thesis already fired (+226%); upside now hinges on a capacity/run-rate ramp that is execution-heavy and largely in consensus ($77 avg, $65-85 range). Asymmetry skews to downside; calibrated win 0.45.

LW.valuation_basis: Activist (Starboard) lane scored as a value-gap re-rate, not a deal: Starboard (disclosed ~3/8/2026) filed a named slate (Cobb + Smith) and a concrete ~$500M total cost-reduction demand (vs co's $250M-by-FY28 plan), Jana already holds board seats; NO settlement yet and the FY2026 annual-meeting date is not set. Model as spread to the activist-success value. deal_price $52 = fair_value_target = where LW re-rates if the $500M reset is adopted: TTM EBITDA ~$894M (depressed off ~$1.4B peak); restoring ~$400-500M of EBITDA toward ~$1.25-1.3B at ~8.5x EV/EBITDA, net debt ~$3.9B (interest exp $177M/qtr), ~139M sh -> ~$48-55 equity. undisturbed_price $40 = pre-Starboard trough / no-action standalone. break_prob 0.45 = odds the campaign forces the reset/refresh by next meeting. Live $42.5 vs Street $49.6. Sources: WSJ/usnews 3/8/2026; marketscreener; LW Q3 FY26 10-Q (sec.gov 4/1/2026).

MAT.valuation_basis: Southeastern/Longleaf (>4%) public sale/buyout call 5/7/26 at trough $14 (52wk low $13.99); PE/Hasbro/media-IP routes floated; board 'will consider' (soft). Take-out SoP: 2026E adj EBITDA ~$1,050M x 10.0x (Hasbro ~11x EV/EBITDA; toy/IP LBO comps 9-11x) = $10,500M EV; less net debt $1,730M (EV-mcap, NetDebt/EBITDA 2.3x) = $8,770M / 290.6M sh = $30.18. FLOOR = de-rated NO-deal standalone at ~5.5x trough EBITDA $1,000M = $5,500M - $1,730M = $3,770M/290.6 = $12.97 (BELOW live $14.21 - mkt prices secular decline, no deal). Southeastern/CEO-comp '>$30 fair value' = advocacy CEILING only. RR=(30.18-14.21)/(14.21-13.0)=13.2 -> highest asymmetry. Src: Businesswire/Southeastern letter 5/7/26, Mattel response 5/8/26, FMP TTM.

sop_components.segment: Toys + IP (consolidated operating business; IP-licensing optionality already in EBITDA)
sop_components.basis: Hasbro (HAS) ~11x EV/EBITDA; recent toy/IP take-private comps 9-11x; 10x = conservative LBO/strategic entry on a stable franchise (Barbie/Hot Wheels/UNO).

FUN.valuation_basis: Activist (Jana) sale-push modeled as spread to a take-private. Jana (Ostfeld, w/ Travis Kelce) public letter 3/17/2026 demanding immediate sale/data room after Cedar Fair+Six Flags $200M synergies stalled; won a new chair (Mar). HIGHLY LEVERED: net debt ~$5.41B (EV $7.70B - mkt $2.28B), net-debt/EBITDA 6.8x, TTM EBITDA ~$800M -> equity is a thin sliver, so a buyout at a higher EV multiple has large leveraged upside AND a fragile floor. deal_price $31 = fair_value_target: a sponsor take-out at ~10.5x EV/EBITDA on ~$850M synergized EBITDA = ~$8.9B EV - $5.41B net debt = ~$3.5B / ~102M sh ~ $34; haircut for leverage/financing risk to $31. undisturbed_price $16.5 = pre-letter level (stock down 60% from $57 post-merger high). break_prob 0.45 = odds a sale/LBO actually closes (annual meeting May 26 PAST -> near-term proxy lever spent; hardens only if board opens a process). Live $22.3 (popped +7.6% today). Sources: financialcontent/JANA 3/17/2026; FMP key-metrics-ttm; FUN 8-K (sec.gov 2026).

VOYA.valuation_basis: Activist (Toms Capital) SOFT sale push modeled as spread to a takeout. Toms/TCIM letter (6/1/2026) urging sale or break-up of the $1.1T pension+insurance platform ($360B actively managed); flags stop-loss health drag (~$10M op loss Q4'25). But NO slate, NO quantified figure, NO date, and stock is AT a 52-wk high ($88.83) -> limited unpriced upside. deal_price $108 = fair_value_target: a strategic/PE takeout near ~11-12x FY27E EPS ~$11.0 (Street FY27E EPS $10.99) ~ $108-117, also = analyst high $117; consolidation comps support it (Janus Henderson taken out $8.6B by Trian-led group, Schroders/Nuveen). undisturbed_price $77 = pre-campaign level (~50-day avg $76.7, near where it sat before the 6/1 news; +2.5% today on the report). break_prob 0.30 = low odds a sale materializes from a soft, unquantified, no-slate campaign at a full price. Live $88.83 vs Street $95.7. Sources: Bloomberg/Hedgeweek/InsuranceJournal Toms 6/1/2026; FMP estimates + key-metrics-ttm.

EA.valuation_basis: Merger-arb INVERSE/skeptic. $210.00 all-cash take-private by Saudi-PIF + Silver Lake + Affinity (Kushner); largest-ever LBO ($55B). HSR cleared; CFIUS is the SOLE remaining gate, contractual outside date Sep 28 2026 (~3.7mo); $1B reverse break fee. Gross spread at $203.06 ref = +3.4% only. SKEPTIC: a Saudi-sovereign acquiring control of a top US media/IP+player-data company is a genuine national-security review, NOT the formality the tight spread implies — Street over-weights the Kushner/Trump political tie. Break_prob set at 18% vs Street's implied ~single digits. fair_value=deal $210; floor=unaffected $168.32 (Sep 25 2025), cushioned by record FY26 standalone results. R:R = (210-203.06)/(203.06-168.32)=0.20 — the inverse thesis: +3.4% up vs -17% down on non-trivial CFIUS break risk. Source: EA IR press release + CreditSights/TIKR (Sep 2025-2026), live FMP quote 2026-06-08.

QRVO.valuation_basis: Merger-arb, mixed cash+stock. Each QRVO -> $32.50 cash + 0.960 SWKS. SWKS live $77.51 -> stock leg 0.960*77.51=$74.41 -> current per-share consideration (deal_price) = $32.50+$74.41 = $106.91. Gross spread at $101.90 ref = +4.9%, long-dated (close late-26/early-27 per SWKS Q2-FY26 call, May 2026). Announced 10/28/2025 implied value $105.31 (SWKS 10/27 close) = +15.14% to QRVO 30-day VWAP. China SAMR Phase II is the SOLE open gate — a US-US RF-chip tie-up held hostage to Beijing amid the chip-export war = the geopolitical swing; SAMR can slow-walk/condition as leverage. break_prob 22%. floor=undisturbed ~$82 (QRVO standalone high-$70s/low-$80s pre-bid; conservative vs the 30-day-VWAP basis ~$91). R:R = (106.91-101.90)/(101.90-82)=0.25; note the stock leg means downside is partly hedged by being long the SWKS-equivalent on break, but I floor to QRVO standalone. Source: Qorvo DEFM14A / 425s + SWKS 8-K (Oct 2025-May 2026), live FMP 2026-06-08.

SKYT.valuation_basis: Merger-arb COLLAR. IonQ buying SkyWater (US defense foundry). Each SKYT -> $15.00 cash + $20.00 IonQ stock; collar 0.3326 sh (if IonQ 20d-VWAP > $60.13) to 0.5265 sh (if < $37.99). IonQ live $63.58 is ABOVE the upper collar -> exchange ratio FIXED at 0.3326 -> stock leg now floats = 0.3326*63.58 = $21.15 -> current face (deal_price) = $15.00+$21.15 = $36.15. SKYT live $36.82 trades THROUGH face (-1.8% to face), so there is NEGATIVE arb carry — the book is long for (a) the floored downside: $15 cash locked + stock leg can only fall back to $20 unless IonQ < $37.99 (i.e. >=$35 face protected across the whole collar band), and (b) above-collar IonQ-beta optionality. FTC 2nd Request issued Apr 24 2026 on the vertical/national-security-adjacent combo (the open gate); target Q2/Q3 2026. break_prob 25% (2nd-request adds real timeline/condition risk). floor=undisturbed $25.36 (38% premium to SKYT 30-day VWAP as of 1/23/2026 implies ~$25.4 standalone). R:R to face = (36.15-36.82)/(36.82-25.36) = -0.06 — captured value is the collar floor + IonQ upside, NOT spread. Source: IonQ/SkyWater 8-Ks + IonQ press release (Jan 2026) + FTC 2nd-request 8-K (Apr 2026), live FMP 2026-06-08.

SEM.valuation_basis: Merger-arb, insider MBO with a MAJORITY-OF-THE-MINORITY wrinkle. $16.50/sh cash take-private led by Ortenzio/Jackson + Welsh Carson (WCAS); ~18% premium; $3.9B EV; insiders ROLL OVER (not offered to public). Conditioned on majority of votes cast by UNAFFILIATED holders; vote scheduled Jun 26 2026. SEM live $16.58 trades THROUGH the $16.50 deal (+$0.08) -> market is pricing a BUMP, fed by multiple fiduciary-breach investigations (BFA Law, May-Jun 2026) giving the minority/appraisal leverage on a low-ball founder buyout. fair_value_target $17.50 = bump-skewed post-catalyst value the tape is sniffing (deal_price contracted floor = $16.50). downside_floor = pre-announcement undisturbed ~$14.00 (200-day $14.89, 52w-low $11.65) — where SEM falls back if the minority votes it down. break/vote-fail prob 20%. R:R = (17.50-16.58)/(16.58-14.00)=0.36 — poor BECAUSE it trades through: capped bump upside vs real vote-fail air-pocket. Source: Select Medical 8-K/DEFA14A + PRNewswire deal release + 13D/A + GlobeNewswire investigation notices (2026), live FMP 2026-06-08.

EXFY.valuation_basis: CONFIRMED capital-return. Modified Dutch-auction self-tender for up to $25.0M of Class A at $0.98-$1.20, expires 12:00am Jun 11 (end of Jun 10 2026); NO minimum, NO financing condition; buys 20.83M-25.51M sh = ~25-30% of Class A float. Live, FORWARD (no amendment/termination). EXFY live $1.175 sits inside the band, so rational clearing price prints at/near the $1.20 CEILING (holders won't tender below market) -> announced_return_per_share = $1.20 (the capturable forward return into the prorate). Because only ~25-30% of float is repurchased, the realized blend to a holder ~ 0.30*$1.20 (prorated tender) + 0.70*stub; residual_value (post-buyback stub level) ~$0.99 (reverts toward 50-day avg once the bid lifts, modestly accretion-helped by the ~25-30% share-count cut). fair_value_target SET to the tender ceiling $1.20 (NOT $1.20+residual, to avoid partial-tender double-count). downside_floor = ex-return $0.98 (the auction MINIMUM). reference_rr = (1.20-1.175)/(1.175-0.98)=0.13 — a near-dated, low-risk small return (the lone survivor): upside capped at $1.20, floored at $0.98, T-2 days. Source: Expensify 8-K + SC TO-I + IR/BusinessWire release (May 13 2026), live FMP 2026-06-08.

AD.valuation_basis: Capital-return + at-market take-private. TDS (owns ~82%) proposal (May 7-8 2026) = 0.86 TDS/AD all-stock; 0.86 x $38.93 TDS (live) = $33.48 stock value. PLUS $11.00 special cash div (record Jun 11, pay Jun 25 2026; funded by $1.0B Verizon spectrum sale closed Jun 1 + $168M T-Mobile) which is declared/near-mechanical, PLUS an assumed $10.40 transaction div (~$900M) the deal requires Array to pay pre-close. announced_return_per_share=$11.00 (the locked special div); residual_value=$43.88 = 0.86xTDS ($33.48) + $10.40 transaction div. fair_value_target=$54.88. Floor=ex-return standalone ~$40 if TDS walks (you still keep the funded $11; lose the at-market stock + transaction div). NOTE deal is AT-MARKET (0.86 ~no premium) so spread is thin and div is largely priced, per context. Source: TDS/AD 8-K & Form 425, May 2026; AD 8-K spectrum-sale/special-div, Jun 2026.
AD.prob_basis: Controlling-holder (82% TDS) take-private at-market; special committee (PJT/Cravath) reviewing. Near-certain the $11 special pays (declared, funded); deal-close ~80% but at-market terms cap spread.

JOF.valuation_basis: CEF discount-capture self-tender. Board self-tender for up to 10% of shares at 98% of NAV; commenced ~Jun 1, expires Jul 1 2026, price date Jul 2 (GlobeNewswire May 22 2026). Estimated live NAV ~$12.75/sh (price $11.41 at JOF's typical ~10% discount; FY N-CSR cited +55.8% NAV). Tender price = 0.98 x $12.75 = ~$12.50, but only 10% of shares clear pro-rata. announced_return_per_share = the discount-capture: tendered slice realizes ~$12.50 vs $11.41 cost (~$1.09/tendered sh), but blended across the book ~$0.11/sh. residual_value = the un-tendered 90% stub that keeps trading at a persistent discount (~$11.40). fair_value_target = blended 0.10x$12.50 + 0.90x$11.41 = $11.52. Floor = ex-tender residual-discount level ~$11.20 (discount does NOT close; it persists/widens once the catalyst passes). Thin-edge NAV arithmetic, not a re-rate. Source: GlobeNewswire/StockTitan, May 2026.
JOF.prob_basis: Board-approved fixed-mechanic tender at 98% NAV; execution near-certain. Edge is pure discount-capture on the 10% pro-rata slice; residual discount persists, so blended uplift is small.

RARE.valuation_basis: UX111 (Sanfilippo MPS IIIA gene therapy) PDUFA Sep 19 2026. CRITICAL: this is NOT a cash-shell binary -- RARE has a $730-760M product-revenue base (Crysvita $500-520M, Dojolvi $100-110M, Evkeeza), $534M cash, 96.6M sh. So 'loss' floor = the standalone multi-product business, NOT cash, and a 2nd CRL is a delay not a zero. Jul-2025 CRL was CMC/manufacturing-only ('no review issues related to the clinical data package'); BLA resubmission accepted Apr 2026 with 8-yr durable data + US-manufacturing fix (Andelyn/Bedford). win_prob 0.72 (CMC-only Class-2 resubmission base-rate ~70-85%). target_on_win $30 = ~$19-20/sh standalone base business (~2.5x $745M sales rare-disease comp) + ~$8-10/sh risk-adj UX111 (peak sales midpoint ~$300-400M; range Leerink $150M to bull $1B). downside_floor $18 = standalone base ex-UX111 (going concern; cash $5.5/sh + product franchise). Note 20-analyst consensus 12mo target $64 reflects the FULL pipeline (Angelman/OI separate catalysts), not just this event. Source: Ultragenyx PR Apr 2/May 2026; Q1-2026 10-Q.
RARE.prob_basis: CMC-only CRL (no clinical-data issues) + accepted resubmission + 8yr durable efficacy + onshore manufacturing fix. CMC/Class-2 resubmission historical approval ~70-85%; set 0.72. Downside bounded by $730M+ commercial base, so EV-positive even with modest win_prob.

COGT.valuation_basis: Bezuclastinib (NonAdvSM) PDUFA Dec 30 2026 -- approval ~priced (SUMMIT highly stat-sig, met all primary+key-secondary, FDA: no AdCom, no review issues). The UNPRICED leg is M&A scarcity: Sanofi bought the ONLY systemic-mastocytosis comp (Blueprint/Ayvakit, ~$9.1-9.5B, closed 2025), leaving bezu as the sole independent SM asset; Ayvakit SM peak guided >$2B. Cash $866M (into 2028), ~171M sh (~$5/sh cash). win_prob 0.90 (clean SUMMIT, no AdCom). target_on_win $48 = strategic re-rate: SM+GIST combined peak ~$1.5-2B at ~4-4.5x peak-sales takeout precedent (Sanofi/Ayvakit) -> ~$5-7B EV, net of cash ~$35-45/sh standalone-on-approval; M&A premium is the call option above. Consensus median $55 (range $35-72). downside_floor $14 = cash ~$5/sh + residual GIST/AdvSM pipeline value (PEAK GIST Ph3 also positive) on a surprise CRL. Source: Cogent PR Mar/May 2026; FiercePharma (Sanofi/Blueprint); Cogent Q1-2026 8-K.
COGT.prob_basis: SUMMIT pivotal highly statistically significant; FDA confirmed no AdCom and no identified review issues. Approval prob ~0.90. The real edge is M&A scarcity post-Sanofi/Blueprint, which target_on_win captures partially and is upside-skewed.

SVRA.valuation_basis: MOLBREEVI (molgramostim, autoimmune PAP) PDUFA Nov 22 2026 (priority review; extended +3mo from Aug, FDA flagged NO safety/efficacy/CMC concerns -- de-risking). First-and-ONLY aPAP therapy = orphan monopoly. Single-asset co: cash $203M + $150M non-dilutive on approval, 204.9M sh (~$0.99/sh cash). Ph3 IMPALA-2 positive (improved exercise distance/duration). win_prob 0.85 (positive Ph3 + priority review + FDA explicitly no-concerns; extension administrative). target_on_win $8 = orphan re-rate toward the $9-11/sh strategic zone (sell-side notes a pulmonary-franchise acquirer e.g. Roche could justify $9-11 pre-approval); peak sales $150-250M ($400-500K/yr, 500-1,000 US pts +EU/UK) at ~4x peak -> ~$800M EV +cash; haircut for mgmt's stated go-it-alone commercialization. downside_floor $1.50 = ~cash on a CRL (single-asset, minimal residual). Source: Savara Q1-2026 PR/10-Q; StockTitan Nov-22 PDUFA; BusinessWire MHRA/EMA filings 2026.
SVRA.prob_basis: Positive Ph3 IMPALA-2 + priority review + FDA explicitly flagged no safety/efficacy/CMC concerns (3-mo extension was administrative). First/only aPAP therapy. Approval prob ~0.85; downside near cash given single-asset structure widens the barbell.

BLCO.valuation_basis: BHC's ~88% stake overhangs BLCO, suppressing it to ~1.0x EV/sales vs ~3x eye-care peers (Wells Fargo: overhang explains the gap). Catalyst = BHC distributes/monetizes the stake (pending leverage targets + Valeant fraudulent-transfer litigation tail; GMO case settled). SoP by segment on EV/sales (FY25: Vision Care $2,923M, Pharma $1,284M, Surgical $894M): VC 3.0x=$8,769M, Pharma 2.8x=$3,595M, Surgical 3.3x=$2,950M; sum EV $15,314M (=14.9x 2026E adj EBITDA $1,025M, between Cooper 10.9x and medtech median 15.8x). Less net debt $5,000M (Q4'25 $4,987M; Q1'26 -229M is a filing glitch w/ LT debt=0) and +$700M adj (holdco/overhang-liquidation leakage + litigation tail) = $9,614M / 356.5M sh = $26.97. FLOOR = no-deal at 9x EBITDA $1,025M = $9,225M - $5,000M = $4,225M/356.5 = $11.85 (just below 52wk low $11.44; a BHC block-sale can push through it). RR=(26.97-15.17)/(15.17-11.8)=3.5. Src: BLCO 10-K/4Q25 8-K + 2026 guide ($5.375-5.475B rev, $1.0-1.05B adj EBITDA), Cooper Cos 10.9x/3.5x (5/24/26).

sop_components.segment: Vision Care (contact lenses, dry-eye/MIEBO, lens care, OTC)
sop_components.basis: Cooper Companies (COO) ~3.5x EV/sales, Alcon vision-care ~4x; haircut to 3.0x for BLCO's lower segment margin.
Specialty eye-pharma peers ~3x EV/sales; 2.8x reflects mixed branded/generic and patent-cliff risk on legacy lines.
Alcon surgical segment ~3.5-4x EV/sales; 3.3x reflects enVista IOL recall overhang.

AMLX.valuation_basis: Bio convergence / barbell on avexitide (GLP-1 receptor antagonist) Phase 3 LUCIDITY in post-bariatric hypoglycemia. FDA-agreed composite Level 2/3 hypoglycemia endpoint, Breakthrough Therapy designation, enrollment completed (78 pts randomized, last dosed Mar 2026), topline anticipated Q3 2026, launch 2027 (Amylyx PR, 2026). Q1-2026 cash $279.8M into 2028, ~110M shares (~$2.54/sh cash). win_prob 0.62 from BTD + FDA-agreed endpoint + prior Ph2b signal, tempered by single small Ph3. target_on_win $28 (H.C. Wainwright PT; Goldman Buy >$1B US peak, BofA $1.8B peak); downside_on_loss $4.00 = cash floor + residual pipeline (AMX0114/AMX0035) value, near 52wk low $4.76. EV = 0.62*28 + 0.38*4.00 = $18.88 vs $12.85 ref = +46.9%.
AMLX.prob_basis: FDA-agreed composite L2/L3 endpoint + Breakthrough Therapy designation + prior Ph2b avexitide signal; haircut for single ~78-pt pivotal; topline Q3 2026 (Amylyx 8-K 2026; H.C. Wainwright/Goldman/BofA coverage).

CELC.valuation_basis: Bio convergence / largely DE-RISKED catalyst: priority-review PDUFA July 17, 2026 (~6 wks out) for gedatolisib (PI3K/mTOR) wild-type NDA in HR+/HER2- advanced breast cancer; launch prep Q3 2026. This is an APPROVAL date, not a trial readout - VIKTORIA-1 mutant topline (May 1) and detailed mutant + wild-type efficacy (Jun 2, 2026) both positive with statistically significant PFS vs alpelisib+fulvestrant (Quiver/StocksToTrade 2026), so the binary is regulatory/label rather than efficacy. win_prob 0.85. target_on_win $153 (FMP consensus; Craig-Hallum $189, HCW/Guggenheim $165). downside_on_loss $60 = CRL/label-narrowing pullback to pre-data level (NOT cash $3/sh, since wild-type data already supports value; 48.35M sh, $145M cash into 2027). EV = 0.85*153 + 0.15*60 = $139.05 vs $88.34 ref = +57.4%.
CELC.prob_basis: Priority-review PDUFA 2026-07-17 with BOTH VIKTORIA-1 cohorts (mutant + wild-type) already positive on PFS; binary is approval/label not efficacy, hence high calibrated p (FDA filing + topline May/Jun 2026).

DFTX.valuation_basis: Bio convergence / barbell on DT120 ODT (MM120 / lysergide D-tartrate; co. rebranded MindMed->Definium Jan 2026, NASDAQ:DFTX). Three pivotal Ph3 readouts in 2026: nearest binary = Emerge (MDD) topline late Q2 2026, then Voyage + Panorama (GAD) Q3 2026 (Definium PR/8-K 2026). JAMA-published Ph2 dose-dependent MM120 GAD efficacy de-risks mechanism; FDA Breakthrough designation. Q1-2026 cash $373.4M, 109.1M shares (~$3.42/sh cash). win_prob 0.55 for the nearest Emerge MDD readout - functional-unblinding risk in psychedelic Ph3 caps p despite strong Ph2 signal. target_on_win $36 (FMP consensus; high $48) reflecting MDD + remaining GAD optionality. downside_on_loss $8.00 = cash $3.42 + preserved value of the still-pending GAD program (Voyage/Panorama not yet read). EV = 0.55*36 + 0.45*8.00 = $23.40 vs $22.53 ref = +3.9%.
DFTX.prob_basis: FDA BTD + JAMA-published Ph2 dose-dependent MM120 efficacy support mechanism; functional-unblinding/placebo risk in psychedelic Ph3 caps calibrated p at the nearest Emerge MDD readout (late Q2 2026).

FIP.valuation_basis: Distressed/deleveraging recovery on FTAI Infrastructure post-Long Ridge sale. Definitive deal (FTAI/MARA IR, SEC 8-K, TradingView, Apr 29-30 2026): MARA buys Long Ridge for $1.512B base, expected close Q3-2026 on FERC/HSR/STB approval; proceeds wipe $1.16B Long-Ridge asset debt and repay ~$300M parent debt (>$300M net proceeds, ~$30M/yr interest saved). Remaining SOP run-rate EBITDA: Rail/Transtar+Wheeling ~$165M (Q1 $40.2M annualized + $23M cost-save target), Jefferson Terminal target >$100M (current ~$60M), Repauno Phase 2 ~$80M (online late-2026/2027). Apply ~9x infra/terminal multiple to a near-term ramp ~$280-300M run-rate -> EV ~$2.6B; less pro-forma parent debt ~$1.05B and pref/holdco ~$0.3B -> equity ~$1.25B / 118.16M sh ~ $10.6 at full ramp. recovery_value haircut to $9.00 for Jefferson/Repauno execution-ramp risk (consensus PT $11.67, high $13). instrument_ref = FIP common equity. downside_floor = deal-break/current trough $3.90 (52wk low). RR=(9.00-4.511)/(4.511-3.90)=7.35.

MGM.valuation_basis: Minority-squeeze merger-arb, all-cash but PRE-AGREEMENT. People Inc. (formerly IAC; 26.1% holder via Barry Diller) tabled a NON-BINDING $48.30/share all-cash proposal Jun 1 2026 (~$18B incl. debt) to take the remaining 73.9% private; 24.1% premium to 30-day VWAP, 10.6% premium to the prior close. Board confirmed receipt Jun 1 and is reviewing with advisors -> no signed agreement, no price certainty. deal_price=$48.30; undisturbed=$43.67 ($48.30/1.106, last close before the bid). At live $47.21 the stock already sits ~98% of the headline bid (a sell-side analyst argues the board should demand more), so the spread is thin and skewed: limited arb capture to $48.30, an embedded BUMP option above it, against a real no-deal/standalone reversion to ~$43.67. CAPR.valuation_basis: BARBELL, FDA PDUFA Aug-22-2026 for deramiocel in DMD cardiomyopathy. CRL (CMC/manufacturing) LIFTED Mar-2026; FDA accepted Class-2 resubmission, has 'not identified any potential review issues', GMP facility operational; Phase-3 HOPE-3 hit BOTH primary skeletal (PUL v2.0, 54% slowing, p=0.029) and key cardiac (LVEF, 91% slowing, p=0.041). win_prob 0.70 -> target $52 (mid of $50-63 analyst bull range contemplating approval; Nippon Shinyaku commercial partner). loss -> ~$7 cash floor (cash+STI $278.6M Q1-26 / 'robust $318M' cited / 45.72M sh = $6.1-7.0 + residual pipeline). Live $26.44: P(win)*52 + P(loss)*7 = $38.5 EV (+45.6%). Sources: Capricor PR (capricor.com) 2026-03-12 HOPE-3, 2026 new-PDUFA PR, Q1-26 10-Q.
CAPR.prob_basis: win_prob 0.70: GlobalData-style pre-registration PTSR for rare-disease/chronic-urticaria-class Ph3 assets ~65-75%; here de-risked by a positive dual-endpoint Ph3 (HOPE-3), an already-lifted CRL, and FDA's 'no review issues' statement, haircut modestly for prior CMC/CRL history and autologous cell-therapy manufacturing complexity. EV-positive barbell, NOT a smooth ratio: +97% on approval vs -74% on CRL/failure to ~cash.

CLDX.valuation_basis: BARBELL, two unfired binaries on barzolvolimab: PN Ph2 topline summer-2026 and pivotal Ph3 CSU topline Q4-2026 (EMBARQ-CSU1/2, 1,939 pts enrolled, ~6mo ahead of schedule, Feb-25-2026 PR). Value driver = Ph3 CSU. Ph2 CSU already best-in-disease (UAS7=0 complete response up to 51% @12wk deepening to 71% @52wk, durable off-drug). win_prob 0.72 -> target $55 (analyst median $53 / Wells Fargo $54 OW; ~$12B peak-market framing). loss -> ~$8 floor (cash+STI $451.5M Q1-26 / 66.54M sh = $6.79 + residual ColdU/SD-Ph3 & PN/AD pipeline optionality). Live $28.48: P(win)*55 + P(loss)*8 = $41.8 EV (+46.9%). Sources: Celldex Q1 update 2026-05-07, enrollment-complete PR 2026-02-25, EAACI/AAAAI 2025-26 Ph2 data.
CLDX.prob_basis: win_prob 0.72: GlobalData 67% Ph3->pre-registration PTSR for chronic-urticaria class, nudged up for the unusually strong, durable Ph2 CSU readthrough (deep complete-response, maintained off-therapy) that substantially de-risks the registrational Ph3. EV-positive barbell: +93% on a clean Ph3 CSU win vs -72% to ~cash on failure; multi-shot pipeline (PN summer, ColdU/SD Ph3, AD) cushions the floor.

Trades $12.77 << $14.23 floor-of-implied, so market is discounting the unlisted stub + bank-merger (Fed) approval risk. Definitive proxy filed May 8 2026; special meeting Jun 23 2026; outside date Nov 23 2026; $27M/$40M reverse break fees. Undisturbed = $11.80 (Nov 21 2025 close).

GTLS.valuation_basis: Clean late-stage cash arb: Baker Hughes (BKR) acquires Chart at $210.00/sh CASH (Merger Agreement Jul 28 2025; replaced terminated Flowserve all-stock MOE that valued GTLS at $159.98, $266M break fee paid). Shareholders approved Oct 6 2025; HSR waiting period EXPIRED Nov 6 2025; only gate left is EU Phase I, Form CO filed May 21 2026 with provisional EC deadline Jun 26 2026 (no SIEC concerns flagged), close guided July 2026 (8-K, stocktitan / mlex / SEC). fair_value_target = deal_price $210. HLX.valuation_basis: All-stock REVERSE merger (Agreement Apr 22 2026, announced Apr 23): PRIVATE Hornbeck Offshore is the acquirer; combined co = Hornbeck name, ticker HOS. Fixed ratio 10.27167 HLX sh per Hornbeck sh. Pro forma: Hornbeck holders ~55%, HLX holders ~45% fully diluted; $75M+ synergies; two-step Delaware reincorporation (SEC S-4 / 8-K / Seeking Alpha). NO acquirer market price (Hornbeck private) so this is NOT a priced spread -> fair value derived from implied combined entity. HLX mkt cap ~$1.48B (152M sh x $9.715) at 45% implies combined equity ~$3.29B. NO cash premium; the only value-add to HLX holders is their 45% share of $75M synergies: cap at ~5x OSV EV (no clean listed pure-comp -> WIDEN floor) = $375M, HLX 45% = ~$169M = ~$1.10/sh on top of standalone ~$9.70 -> fair_value_target ~$11.00 post-close re-rate of the larger synergized OSV co. Floor = undisturbed standalone ~$8.50 (pre-Apr-22). Needs HLX vote + regulatory; close 2H-2026.

IMMX.valuation_basis: BARBELL (not ratio) on NXC-201 (NEXICART-2), first-in-class CAR-T for relapsed/refractory AL Amyloidosis. Interim 95% CR (19/20), zero relapses-to-CR; FDA BTD + RMAT + ODD (US/EU). BLA-enabling pivotal, enrollment complete (8-K Mar 30 2026); next update late Sept 2026 (May 21 2026 PR, globenewswire/immixbio). Post-May-21 $150M raise (16.78M sh @ $8.94, ~$140.65M net) + ~$91M (Mar 31) = ~$232M cash, runway to mid-2028; 54.4M sh out (10-Q). win_prob CALIBRATED 0.70 (HIGH for bio, justified: 95% CR interim on a pivotal + BTD/RMAT + orphan + NO approved competitor in r/r ALA). target_on_win $18: peak sales $700M-$1B+ (ALA TAM $1.4-2.8B, one-and-done at $300-500K/dose); risk-adj re-rate to ~$1.0-1.2B EV / 54.4M sh + cash ~ low-to-mid $20s on full success, haircut to $18 for a Sept-interim (not approval) catalyst. downside_on_loss $4.00 ~ net cash ($232M/54.4M=$4.27) less burn discount. EV(0.70x18 + 0.30x4.0)=$13.80 vs $7.77 ref = +78% expected.
IMMX.prob_basis: Interim 95% CR (19/20) on the BLA-enabling pivotal NEXICART-2 + FDA BTD/RMAT + US/EU orphan designation; first-in-class with no approved competitor in relapsed/refractory AL amyloidosis. Late-stage pivotal readouts with breakthrough-grade interim efficacy historically clear far above the ~50% genericized Ph3 base rate; calibrated 0.70.

MOD.valuation_basis: OVERRIDE of merger_arb lane: this is an RMT spin, not a takeout of MOD. Per the Jan-29-2026 agreement (8-K) Modine carves its Performance Technologies segment into Gentherm (THRM); MOD holders KEEP RemainCo Modine (Climate Solutions) and ALSO receive ~21M THRM shares, and Modine receives a $210M cash distribution. RemainCo = Climate Solutions (FY2026 adj EBITDA $377.1M, sales $2,062M; Data Centers +60-80%/HVAC +5-10% into FY2027) less ~$50M retained corporate -> ~$420M FY2027E EBITDA at 22.0x (rich data-center thermal, between nVent ~17x and Vertiv ~28x) = $9,240M. THRM stake = 21M sh x $36.77 live = $772M. Net debt $362.8M (mgmt) less $210M cash in = $153M; +$120M retained cap leases. 53M sh (52.82M out, 10-K). Build=(10,012-153-120)/53=$183.75. HONEST READ: MOD at $275 (52wk low $86 -> +220%) already prices RemainCo at ~33x fwd EBITDA; even a generous SoP sits well below spot -> NO upside / catalyst over-discounted. PT contributed at ~6.8x $147M post-synergy EBITDA = $1.0B EV / $790M equity (deal terms).

NGNE.valuation_basis: Dated forward binary on the mid-2026 Phase 1/2 NGN-401 interim readout (>=12-mo follow-up on all 10 pediatric/adolescent participants); Embolden registrational dosing completed 2026-06-07 with 25 dosed (vs 24 ITT target), Embolden topline not until 2H-2027, BLA after. WIN ($42): a clean, durable 12-mo efficacy+safety readout re-rates above the 52-wk high ($37.27) as the Rett gene-therapy opportunity (no approved disease-modifying Rx) de-risks into registration; BTD already granted on the Oct-30-2025 cut (88% CGI-I improving, 35 milestones, durable multidomain gains). LOSS ($12): durability fade / SAE / endpoint ambiguity collapses toward the ~$7.28/sh net-cash floor (cash+ST inv $243.2M, net cash ~$115M / 15.80M sh) plus residual fully-dosed-registrational + platform optionality. BARBELL not a ratio. Sources: Neurogene Q1-2026 release (SEC, 2026-05-12), Embolden dosing-complete PR (StockTitan, 2026-06-07), BTD announcement (BioSpace/IR).
NGNE.prob_basis: Calibrated ~0.60 for a clearly stock-re-rating positive interim: high base rate that the mid-2026 update CONFIRMS, since FDA Breakthrough Therapy was already granted on this exact program's earlier interim cut and the construct uses miRNA-regulated EXACT tech to avoid the MECP2-overexpression toxicity that sank prior Rett gene-therapy attempts; haircut from PDUFA-grade certainty because this is an open-label, single-arm, n=10 12-mo durability readout (real fade/safety/endpoint-interpretation variance), not an approval. Sources: Neurogene IR BTD release; Q1-2026 8-K.

OGN.valuation_basis: Definitive all-cash merger: Sun Pharma to acquire Organon at $14.00/sh ($11.75B EV), signed 2026-04-26 (PREM14A on EDGAR + Organon/Sun PRs + MLex). Outside date 2027-01-26; expected close EARLY 2027 (~7-9 months out) pending Organon stockholder vote + US/non-US antitrust + foreign-investment clearances; Sun secured a ~$12B bridge (financing locked); guidance/calls suspended. deal_price=$14.00; undisturbed=$6.86 (2026-04-09 unaffected close, implied by the announced 103% premium). Gross spread at $13.38 = $0.62 (4.6%) over a long, regulatory-gated timeline -> reflects cross-border antitrust/FII + duration risk, partly offset by limited US therapeutic overlap (women's health/biosimilars/established brands) and locked all-cash financing. ~262.6M sh out. Sources: Organon 8-K/PREM14A (StockTitan/EDGAR, Apr 2026); Sun Pharma PR (BusinessWire, 2026-04-26).

PRA.valuation_basis: Definitive all-cash merger: The Doctors Company to acquire ProAssurance at $25.00/sh (~$1.3B), signed Mar-2025; shareholders approved Jun-24-2025; FTC HSR waiting period terminated Jul-2-2025. Per the 2026-06-02 8-K, ALL required insurance-regulator approvals received (AL, DC, IL, MO, PA, TX, VT + Lloyd's + Cayman CIMA) EXCEPT California (pending; timing uncertain but the sole remaining gate); company continues to anticipate closing BY 2026-06-30. deal_price=$25.00; undisturbed=$15.63 (implied by the announced ~60% premium to the 2025-03-18 close). Gross spread at $24.695 = $0.305 (1.24%) over ~3 weeks -> near-certain, near-term close; only break risk is a single in-state insurance regulator on an already 8-jurisdiction-cleared, FTC-cleared deal. ~51.6M sh out. Sources: PRA 8-K (SEC/StockTitan, 2026-06-02); Doctors Company PR (BusinessWire, 2025-03-19).

PTGX.valuation_basis: Dated forward binary on the Q3-2026 rusfertide PDUFA (Priority Review granted 2026-03-02; NDA filed by partner Takeda). Approval mechanically unlocks Protagonist's already-exercised (Apr-2026) opt-out ladder: +$200M on approval +$75M milestone = +$275M cash (~+$4.28/sh on 64.3M sh), plus worldwide royalties 14-29% on $1-2B peak-sales rusfertide and up to $975M total milestone ladder. LOSS ($62): a CRL strips rusfertide but PTGX retains ~$2.86/sh net cash (cash+ST inv $547.8M + LT inv $72.8M, net cash ~$184M incl. the $200M opt-out already received) plus the J&J-partnered icotrokinra (Phase 3 psoriasis/IBD) + obesity pipeline, which underpin ~low-$60s ex-rusfertide -> ~analyst low ($65). BARBELL: EV = 0.88x118 + 0.12x62 = $111.28 (~+14.6% vs $97.135). Sources: Takeda/Protagonist NDA-acceptance PR (BusinessWire/Takeda, 2026-03-01); opt-out PR (StockTitan/BioSpace, Apr-2026); VERIFY Ph3 (77% vs 33% response, ASH-2025); Q1-2026 10-Q balance sheet.
PTGX.prob_basis: Calibrated ~0.88 for FDA approval at the Q3-2026 PDUFA: Phase 3 VERIFY hit hard (77% vs 33% response, met key secondaries incl. phlebotomy reduction) on top of 4-yr REVIVE/THRIVE safety; carries Breakthrough + Priority Review + Fast Track + Orphan; first-in-class hepcidin mimetic with mechanism de-risked by years of Phase 2; partner Takeda (large-cap) filed and committed. ~90% is the empirical approval rate for BTD assets with a positive pivotal under Priority Review; small haircut for first-in-class novelty/CMC. Sources: VERIFY/ASH-2025 data; FDA Priority-Review grant 2026-03-02.

REZI.valuation_basis: Board-committed spin of ADI Global Distribution (Form 10 filed 2026-05-11; 10-12B/A on file; 100% of ADI distributed pro-rata; ADIG ticker reserved). SoP: RemainCo Products & Solutions (branded smart-home/building products, 2025 Standalone Adj EBITDA $581M @ 20.3% margin) at 12.0x (below Allegion ~15x; mature mid-single-digit grower) = $6,972M; ADIG SpinCo (low-margin distribution, 2025 Adj EBITDA $318M @ 6.6%) at 7.0x (Wesco ~9x, ADI lower-margin) = $2,226M. Sum EV $9,198M. Net debt $3,078M (Q1-2026 10-Q: $3,516M debt incl $276M cap lease less $438M cash). Adjustments +$1,082M = $482M convertible preferred (CD&R/Honeywell, ahead of common) + ~$600M NPV of the Honeywell Reimbursement (indemnity) liability retained by RemainCo (~$140M/yr cap). 151M sh. Build=(9,198-3,078-1,082)/151=$33.36; spin unlocks conglomerate discount as P&S becomes a clean branded-products pure-play. Floor=spin-stalls conglomerate-discount levered name ~8.5x blended w/ Honeywell overhang, toward lower half of 52wk $20.41-$45.29.

sop_components.basis: 2025 Standalone Adj EBITDA $581M @ 20.3% margin; 12x vs Allegion ~15x / Carrier resi ~13x (REZI lower growth)

sop_components.basis: 2025 Adj EBITDA $318M @ 6.6% margin; Wesco ~9x, ADI lower-margin pure distribution -> 7x

SRRK.valuation_basis: BARBELL on apitegromab SMA. Catalyst: BLA accepted with FDA Priority Review, PDUFA 2026-09-30; EMA MAA accepted (decision mid-2026). Phase 3 SAPPHIRE MET primary endpoint (HFMSE +1.8 pts, p=0.0192; >=3pt responders 30.4% vs 12.5% placebo) and was PUBLISHED in Lancet Neurology, so efficacy is de-risked and remaining risk is regulatory/CMC, not the drug working. win_prob 0.90; target_on_win $62 (consensus post-approval target, ~5x risk-adj peak sales; Street peak $1.5-2.0B per Jefferies $1.8B / JPM >$2B); downside_on_loss $14 (well above ~$4.0/sh gross cash and ~$1.9/sh net cash on the $200M convert, retaining the broad TGF-beta pipeline + eventual-resubmission optionality since efficacy is already proven). EV = 0.90*62 + 0.10*14 = $57.2 vs $43.46 ref => +32% expected value.
SRRK.prob_basis: Phase 3 SAPPHIRE hit primary (p=0.0192) and is published in Lancet Neurology; FDA granted Priority Review and accepted the BLA; Jefferies models 90% probability-of-success for the age 2+ population. Remaining risk is regulatory/manufacturing timing, not clinical efficacy.

THRM.valuation_basis: RMT, NOT a cash takeout of THRM (override): Gentherm is the surviving acquirer of Modine's Performance Technologies SpinCo (definitive agreements 2026-01-29, tax-free RMT, Q4-2026 target close; needs THRM shareholder vote + IRS PLR + tax conditions). So 'deal_price' here = post-close PRO-FORMA value per current THRM share, not cash. Pro-forma day-one: ~$2.6B combined revenue, $320M adj-EBITDA, ~1.0x net leverage, ~$25M synergies (path to >$520M EBITDA by 2030); former Modine holders ~40% (flexes to >=50.5% for tax). Combined $345M synergy-adj EBITDA x ~12.5x (THRM standalone trades ~11.8x: EV $1.22B = $1.125B mkt-cap + $96M Q1'26 net debt, on $103M FY25 EBITDA; modest re-rate for scale + data-center thermal mix) = ~$4,313M EV - ~$320M PF net debt = ~$3,993M equity / ~50.8M PF shares ~ $79 bull. fair_value_target set conservatively at $44 (partial re-rate; stock already ran to $36.70 near 52wk-high pricing much of the accretion). Floor = undisturbed ~$30 (50-day $30.45, pre-Jan-29 level).

TWO.valuation_basis: Contested merger-arb. Board-backed deal = CrossCountry $12.00/sh ALL-CASH + a small stub dividend (amended merger agreement; no financing contingency; 41 of 53 regulatory approvals secured); special meeting reconvenes 2026-06-11. Competing UWMC $12.50 (cash-or-2.3328 UWMC shares) was UNANIMOUSLY REJECTED by the TWO board as illusory because a stockholder failing to elect cash defaults to UWMC stock worth ~$7.58. Live price $12.345 already trades ABOVE the $12.00 committed cash, i.e. the market is pricing a topping/bump beyond the board deal. deal_price set at $12.25 (CrossCountry $12.00 + ~$0.25 stub, probability-weighted toward a modest topping vs UWMC's $12.50), undisturbed $9.92 (2026-12-16, day before the original UWMC bid). At $12.345 the arb to the committed deal is slightly NEGATIVE - you are paying up for the bidding-war outcome. NAV cushion: book value ~$14.5/sh (price/book ~0.85), so a break likely re-rates toward ~0.7x BV (~$10-11), not a crash.

TXNM.valuation_basis: Clean take-private merger-arb. Blackstone Infrastructure $61.25/sh ALL-CASH ($11.5B EV; agreement May-2025; shareholder vote passed Aug-28-2025). Cleared: FERC (Feb-2026, found consistent with public interest), PUCT (unanimous settlement), HSR (expired), FCC. Remaining: NMPRC (New Mexico - the gating item, historically tough; NM rejected the Avangrid/PNM merger in 2021) and NRC license transfer. deal_price $61.25; undisturbed $50.00 (Blackstone bought 8M new shares at $50; pre-leak 30-day VWAP ~$49.80 as of 2025-03-05, the $61.25 being a 23% premium to it). break_prob 0.18 reflecting the real NMPRC tail despite all other clearances. Gross spread $2.065 (3.5%) to an ~H2-2026 close.

UNF.valuation_basis: Merger-arb. Cintas/UNF definitive agreement signed 3/10/26 (DEFM14A; businesswire 3/10/26). Consideration = $155.00 cash + 0.7720 CTAS/sh. At live CTAS $176.26 the stock leg = 0.7720 x 176.26 = $136.07, so deal_price = 155.00 + 136.07 = $291.07/sh (announce-day headline was $310 when CTAS=$200.77; value floats with CTAS). Shareholder vote 6/11/26; Croatti family ~2/3 of votes locked via voting/support agreement, so vote outcome ~certain. Only open gate = HSR + foreign antitrust; close expected H2 2026. Limited product overlap concerns -> low break risk. Ref UNF $266.29 -> gross spread 9.3%.
UNF.valuation_basis_note: deal_price floats with CTAS; recompute at execution.
UNF.prob_basis: Signed definitive + controlling-family lock-up = vote certain; only HSR/foreign antitrust open on a low-overlap uniform/linen combo -> ~8% break (regulatory tail only). Source: DEFM14A 3/10/26, vote 6/11/26.

VAL.valuation_basis: Merger-arb (all-stock scheme of arrangement). Transocean/Valaris BCA signed 2/9/26 (globenewswire 2/9/26; PREM14A) at FIXED 15.235 RIG per VAL share, no collar. At live RIG $6.18, deal value = 15.235 x 6.18 = $94.15/sh. Ref VAL $91.94 -> gross spread only 2.4%. KEY RISK: deal is under an EXTENDED DOJ antitrust/second-request review (TipRanks 2026, 'extended DOJ antitrust review') because it combines two of the largest offshore drillers -> elevated break probability and a wide downside. Vote pending; close targeted H2 2026 but antitrust can push/break. Undisturbed VAL close 2/6/26 = $62.41 (deal struck at a 31.6% premium / $82.12 implied when RIG=$5.39).
VAL.prob_basis: All-stock signed BCA, but DOJ second-request/extended antitrust review on a top-2 offshore-driller combination materially raises break risk -> ~22% (vs ~5-8% base for a clean strategic). Floor = undisturbed $62.41 (if blocked, VAL re-rates to standalone; offshore tape has firmed since so $62.41 is conservatively wide, appropriate given no antitrust clarity). Source: PREM14A; DOJ-review reporting 2026.

YUM.valuation_basis: Post-catalyst SoP for YUM-ex-Pizza-Hut. Catalyst = exclusive sale of Pizza Hut to LongRange Capital at $3.6-4.3B (Bloomberg/Reuters/FoxBiz May 29-Jun 1 2026), removing 10-straight-quarters of negative PH comps (-15% div op profit in 2025). Retained KFC+TacoBell+Habit re-rate to full asset-light franchise multiples; PH recognized as after-tax cash (~$3.5B, mid-deal $3.95B less low-basis tax). FY2025 div revenue: KFC $3.54B/TacoBell $3.10B/PH $1.01B/Habit $0.57B; TTM EBITDA $2.832B; net debt $11,265M (EV $51,876M - mktcap $40,611M); 279M dil sh. Build: KFC EBITDA $1,400M*18x + TacoBell $1,150M*22x + Habit $40M*9x + PH after-tax proceeds $3,500M, less corporate-EBITDA-drag adj $1,900M (~ -$158M EBITDA at 12x) less net debt, /279. CATALYST LARGELY PRICED: build $147.65 ~ live $147.34; target $152 reflects modest deleveraging/buyback optionality only.
