Q1-26 rev Life Sci $229M + Clin Dx $364M (~$2.55B FY); 2026 op margin guide 10-12%, adj EBITDA ~$460M. 10x = below-peer-margin life-science-tools comp (higher-quality Danaher/Thermo segs 14-18x); Elliott margin-upside case is the catalyst, not base.

BL.valuation_basis: RE-FRAMED from generic merger-arb to a take-private SPREAD: there is NO signed deal — only a reconstituted Strategic Committee (M&A banker Storm Duncan added 2026-03-10 under the Engaged Capital settlement) empowered to negotiate/recommend a business combination. KEY: the attractive 7x ratio is gated by a LOW break_prob (~0.30) — no deal is signed.
BL.prob_basis: No signed agreement; only a committee with an M&A mandate after the Engaged Capital settlement. SAP already walked once. Strategic/PE appetite real but unconfirmed -> deal-happens probability only ~0.30 (break_prob ~0.70). The 7x raw R:R overstates attractiveness because the floor is thin and probability-of-deal is the binding constraint.

CGON.valuation_basis: Bio binary BARBELL on PIVOT-006 Phase 3 topline (adjuvant intravesical cretostimogene vs surveillance in intermediate-risk NMIBC, RFS primary endpoint, N~364 1:1 open-label, 90+ sites, enrollment completed Sep 2025; topline accelerated to H1 2026 per GlobeNewswire/IR 2026-01-09). win_prob = 0.62 (CALIBRATED): the cretostimogene molecule is already de-risked by POSITIVE pivotal Phase 3 BOND-003 data in HR-NMIBC (high complete-response rates), the IR comparator is mere SURVEILLANCE (a weak control that favors the active arm), and the endpoint is RFS in a well-powered registrational design — together pushing PoS above the ~30-40% Phase-3-oncology base rate. target_on_win $78 (clean PIVOT-006 win + rolling HR-NMIBC BLA momentum re-rates toward/through the $75.5 prior 52-wk high). downside_on_loss $32 — NOT bare cash ($1.1B / 88.2M sh = ~$12.5/sh) because a PIVOT-006-only miss leaves the HR-NMIBC BOND-003 franchise + BLA intact; $32 = cash floor + retained HR program value. EV = 0.62*78 + 0.38*32 = $60.5 vs $53.95 ref -> +12.2% expected value (reference_rr stored as EV%).
CGON.prob_basis: Positive pivotal BOND-003 Phase 3 in HR-NMIBC de-risks the same molecule; surveillance-only comparator in PIVOT-006 favors the active arm; well-powered RFS registrational design (N~364) -> calibrated PoS ~0.62, above the generic ~0.35 Phase-3-onc base rate.

CNTA.valuation_basis: MERGER-ARB (Lilly/Centessa). Definitive scheme of arrangement: $38.00 CASH/sh + 1 CVR (up to $9.00, orexin O2R milestones). CNTA.company_note: fair_value_target $40.40 = $38.00 cash + ~$2.40 risk-adjusted CVR (~27% of $9 max; orexin-2-agonist regulatory milestones, multi-year, uncertain). Spread-to-cash is NEGATIVE.

CRVS.valuation_basis: BIO BINARY (barbell). Soquelitinib (ITK inhibitor, oral) registrational Ph3 NCT06561048 vs physician's-choice belinostat/pralatrexate in r/r PTCL, n=150, PFS primary; Orphan + Fast Track; interim late-2026, completion 2027. CRVS.company_note: BARBELL not ratio. Essentially FAIRLY PRICED at $11.17; edge only if you handicap PoS >0.40 or value AD optionality higher. reference_rr shown as EV% (+0.01).

DBVT.valuation_basis: BIO BINARY (approval/launch risk, efficacy already de-risked). DBVT.company_note: BARBELL. ATTRACTIVE asymmetric barbell: efficacy de-risked, ~$9.5 cash floor limits downside to ~-41% vs ~+112% on win. reference_rr shown as EV% (+0.43).

DEO.valuation_basis: Lane 'distressed' overridden: DEO is an out-of-favor blue chip ($44.5B cap), NOT distressed; EABL/Asahi is a SUBSIDIARY disposal, not a take-out of DEO. EABL sale $2.3bn (signed 2025; ~17x EBITDA; values EABL 100% at ~$4.8bn) -> proceeds used to deleverage (~0.25x off 3.4x net debt/EBITDA, net debt $21.7bn at Dec-2025). Residual = de-rated standalone ex-deal ~$76 (fwd P/E already compressed 19.9x->14.9x). fair_value = $4.14 + $76.0 = $80.1 ~ spot -> CONFIRMS thin asymmetry flagged in context: the deal is signed and already in the price. Floor = continued staples/GLP-1 de-rate to ~13x fwd = ~$70, a notch below 52wk low $72.45 (strips residual deal hope). Sources: Diageo PR 2025-EABL; H1-2026 6-K net debt $21.7bn.

DMAC.valuation_basis: BARBELL. Catalyst = pre-specified DSMB interim on first 200 patients (75%/~150 enrolled per StockTitan/BioSpace; completion guided by end-2026) in ReMEDy2 (NCT05065216), an adaptive Ph2/3 of DM199 (rinvecalinase alfa) in acute ischemic stroke. NOTE the interim is a sample-size re-estimation / futility gate, NOT an efficacy success readout (final N 300-728), so 'win' = clears futility with a favorable signal (no large up-sizing), not approval. win_prob 0.55 (continuation likely but a strongly positive non-SSR outcome is rarer; AIS neuroprotection PoS-to-approval historically ~10-15%). target_on_win $10 re-rate (~$540M cap, prior 52-wk high $10.42, 200-day $7.18). downside_on_loss $2 (futility/large up-size toward cash; Q1-26 cash+STI $51.3M / 53.8M sh = $0.95/sh, plus residual DM199 CKD/PNH optionality). EV = 0.55*10 + 0.45*2 = $6.4 vs $5.54 ref = +15.5% (binary EV%).
DMAC.prob_basis: Adaptive Ph2/3 interim is a continuation/futility gate (DSMB SSR), not go/no-go efficacy; base-rate for 'not stopped + no large up-size' ~0.55. Acute ischemic stroke neuroprotection has a long graveyard (PoS-to-approval ~10-15%); de-risking signal weighted but the binary here is the interim itself. Source: DiaMedica PR / BioSpace May-2026 75% enrollment milestone; FY2025 results.

DYN.valuation_basis: BARBELL on the near catalyst. DYNE-251 (z-rostudirsen, exon-51 DMD) BLA for U.S. Accelerated Approval SUBMITTED Q2 2026 on dystrophin surrogate (event already fired +11%; positive pre-BLA meeting, Breakthrough Therapy, DELIVER n=86 clean safety) -> forward catalyst = FDA acceptance/priority review + Q1-2027 launch; DM1 (z-basivarsen/DYNE-101) REC enrollment complete 3-Jun-2026, topline pushed to Q1-2027, so DM1 is a 2027 event not 2026. Frame the DMD AA approval as the binary: win_prob 0.70 (dystrophin-surrogate AA precedent strong post-Sarepta; BTD + clean safety). target_on_win $26 (DMD commercial + DM1 platform optionality; 52-wk high $25, ~$4.3B mcap). downside_on_loss $11 (CRL hits hard but DM1 alive + Q1-26 net cash $734M = $4.44/sh; recent low $8.06). EV = 0.70*26 + 0.30*11 = $21.5 vs $16.93 = +27% (binary EV%).
DYN.prob_basis: Accelerated Approval on dystrophin surrogate with Breakthrough Therapy + positive pre-BLA meeting; exon-skipping AA precedent (Sarepta) supports ~0.70 for FDA acceptance->approval. Source: Dyne 8-K/PR Q2-2026 BLA submission; 10-Q Mar-2026; 8-K 3-Jun-2026 DM1 REC complete.

Brackets consensus ~$43 (public.com, 6 analysts) and below Scotia $58 (higher-gold deck).

Tier-1 long-life Cu-Au at negative AISC; ~140koz Au + ~65Mlb Cu/yr at full ramp; Cu-Au producers (Lundin) ~6-7x, de-risked as 94% built

ERAS.valuation_basis: BARBELL, and the market is OVERPAYING. Catalyst = ERAS-4001 (pan-KRAS-ON inhibitor) initial Ph1 monotherapy readout (safety/PK/initial efficacy) in KRAS-mut solid tumors, BOREALIS-1, guided H2-2026 (Jan-12-2026 release; reiterated Q1-2026 results 11-May-2026). BUT mcap $3.93B vs $409M cash embeds heavy unproven pan-RAS optionality. win_prob 0.35 (early Ph1 onco PoS to a clean, durable positive monotherapy signal is low; pan-RAS(ON) mechanism unvalidated -> wide uncertainty). target_on_win $24 (re-rate to 52-wk high on franchise validation). downside_on_loss $5 (premium collapses toward residual pipeline incl. naporafenib; cash $1.31/sh, 52-wk low $1.21 pre-rally). EV = 0.35*24 + 0.65*5 = $11.65 vs $12.63 = -7.8% -> NEGATIVE EV at current price; the binary is priced through fair value.
ERAS.prob_basis: Phase 1 oncology initial-efficacy PoS historically low (~10-20% to a thesis-confirming signal); pan-RAS(ON) is a novel, clinically unvalidated mechanism -> 0.35 for a clean positive monotherapy readout that holds the premium. Source: Erasca PR 12-Jan-2026 prioritization/runway; Q1-2026 results 11-May-2026.

DayOne implied equity ~$11.1B from Series C new-issue price (Jan 2026); GDS 19.9% look-through = ~$2.2B (~$11.18/ADS)

GLUE.valuation_basis: Barbell, NOT a ratio (65.1M sh; net cash ~US$627M = ~$9.6/sh after $300M Jan-2026 raise at $24). GLUE.prob_basis: MRT-8102 Ph1 (Jan-7-2026): 85% hsCRP reduction, 94% to <2mg/L, favorable safety; NEK7/NLRP3->IL-6/CRP axis de-risked by CANTOS + ziltivekimab; H2-2026 larger GFORCE-1 CVD readout is the de-risking binary; 0.55 reflects strong biomarker but unproven CV-outcome translation/durability at scale + Phase 2 ASCVD execution risk. Source: GlobeNewswire/StockTitan 2026-01-07, Q1'26 10-Q 2026-05-07.

HTT.valuation_basis: Modified Dutch-auction tender (PRNewswire 2026-05-26, SC TO-I): up to 39M ADSs (~24% of 161.9M out) at $2.80-$3.20, expires Jun-24-2026, NOT financing-conditioned; max outlay $124.8M. Net cash incl ST investments ~US$1,240M (cash RMB7,061M + ST inv RMB2,766M - debt RMB1,582M, Q4'25 FX6.9) = ~$7.66/ADS NCAV vs $2.925 px (~38% of net cash). announced_return_per_share = tender price (likely clears near top $3.20 since NCAV>>range; pro-ration ~24% if oversubscribed). residual_value ~$3.10 = accretive stub (post-tender net cash/share rises to ~$9.07 on 122.9M sh) but persistent China cash-box governance discount. downside_floor $2.44 = ex-return/undisturbed level (pre-announcement, -50% 6mo). fair_value_target anchored to the tender ceiling; NCAV ~$9 is the unrealized hard backstop.

IMNM.valuation_basis: Barbell on a REGULATORY binary (95.6M sh; net cash ~US$579M = ~$6.06/sh, Q1'26). RINGSIDE Ph3 data already fired/re-rated (topline Dec-2025, detailed ASCO May-30-2026: HR 0.16 = 84% risk reduction, ORR 56% vs 9%). NDA for varegacestat (AL102, oral GSI, desmoid tumors) submitted Apr-29-2026; live gate = FDA acceptance -> approval (no FDA acceptance confirmed as of Jun-4). win_prob 0.82: exceptional Ph3 + GSI mechanism de-risked by approved comp Ogsiveo/nirogacestat (SpringWorks, acquired by Merck KGaA ~$3.9B 2025), haircut for filing-acceptance + label/launch path. target_on_win ~$28 = re-rate to/above prior $27.65 high (approved 2nd-in-class desmoid franchise EV ~$1.8B + net cash; SpringWorks/$3.9B desmoid comp). downside_on_loss ~$8 = net cash $6 + pipeline/resubmission residual ~$2.
IMNM.prob_basis: RINGSIDE Ph3 HR 0.16 (84% progression/death risk reduction), ORR 56% vs 9% (ASCO 2026-05-30); NDA filed 2026-04-29; GSI mechanism + desmoid indication de-risked by approved comp Ogsiveo/nirogacestat (SpringWorks acquired by Merck KGaA ~$3.9B, 2025). 0.82 = high approval odds on best-in-class data + regulatory precedent, haircut for acceptance/label/launch. Source: BusinessWire/StockTitan 2026-04-29, Q1'26 8-K 2026-05-12.

IP.valuation_basis: Post-spin SoP: RemainCo North America packaging + retained 20% of SpinCo (merged DS Smith + IP EMEA, dual NYSE/LSE, spin 12-18mo per Jan 29 2026 board plan). NA adj EBITDA mid-guide $2.45B x9.0x (premium NA containerboard; PKG ~9.5-10x, slight discount for IP's lower margins) = $22.05B EV. SpinCo EMEA $950M EBITDA x6.0x = $5.7B EV; less ~$2.5B DS-Smith-deal debt = $3.2B equity, IP 20% = $640M. Net debt to RemainCo = $8,307M total - $2,500M to SpinCo = $5,807M; +$800M pension/standup adj. (22,690 - 5,807 - 800)/529.5 sh = $30.37. Honest read: the $2.5B EMEA impairment + two guidance cuts have re-rated IP to ~ its clean SoP; spin doesn't add value above $32.86 -> NO_UPSIDE/mild downside.

sop_components.basis: NA containerboard; comp Packaging Corp (PKG) ~9.5-10x EV/EBITDA, set 9.0x for IP's structurally lower margins; $2.35-2.5B FY26 guide
EMEA $950M EBITDA x6.0x (European packaging, post $2.5B impairment) = $5.7B EV less ~$2.5B deal debt = $3.2B equity; IP keeps 20% for 12-18mo

KVUE.valuation_basis: Merger-arb (KMB/Kenvue). Per-share consideration = $3.50 cash + 0.14625 KMB; at KMB $98.76 that is $3.50 + 0.14625*98.76 = $17.94. Fixed exchange ratio means KVUE holders bear full KMB downside: deal was worth $21.01 at announce (KMB $115.10, Oct-31-2025) and has bled to ~$17.94 as KMB fell to ~$99. Shareholders approved Jan-29-2026 (99% KVUE / 96% KMB), US HSR cleared Feb-4-2026, but FTC issued a SECOND REQUEST, EC FSR provisional deadline Jun-8-2026, and China SAMR opened a deeper review (asked for more info, May-29-2026). Outside date Nov-2-2026, auto-extends to May-3-2027 for regulatory. Expected close H2-2026. Tylenol/autism litigation is the standalone overhang. Source: Kenvue 8-K (kvue-20260129), KMB 8-K (May-2026), MLex (EU FSR), Seeking Alpha (SAMR), DEFM14A.
KVUE.prob_basis: Break ~12%: binding agreement + both shareholder votes + HSR clearance make a walk unlikely; residual risk is a remote SAMR/EC block or a litigation-driven MAC. Note the bigger risk is not a BREAK but further KMB-equity erosion (80% of consideration is stock) — that lowers realized value even on a clean close.

KOD.valuation_basis: BARBELL on DAYBREAK Phase 3 wet-AMD topline (1-yr primary endpoint, ~690 pts, Sept-2026) reading BOTH Zenkuda/tarcocimab AND KSI-501 (first-in-class anti-IL-6/VEGF-trap bispecific). Tarcocimab arm is materially de-risked: DAYLIGHT already MET non-inferiority vs aflibercept in wet AMD (Jul-2023); the 2023 setback was DME (GLEAM/GLIMMER cataract signal), not wet AMD, and the DR program just won GLOW2 (fired Mar-2026, +19%). KSI-501 mechanism is the upside optionality. Cash $169.5M (Q1-26), net cash ~$113.5M (~$2.14/sh), but burn ~$40M/qtr with a going-concern flag — a win still needs dilutive financing. 52.93M sh. Source: KOD 8-K/10-Q (kod-20260331), PRNewswire Q1-2026, GLOW2 release, DAYLIGHT 2023 PRNewswire.
KOD.prob_basis: win_prob 0.55 (> coin-flip) anchored on the prior positive DAYLIGHT wet-AMD readout for the SAME molecule/indication; a 'win' = tarcocimab arm cleanly re-confirms non-inferiority (fileable wet-AMD package), with KSI-501 success as added upside. EV barbell = 0.55*$50 + 0.45*$9 = $31.55 vs $30.26 ref (+4.3%); asymmetric ($50 up / $9 down) but thin because GLOW2 already re-rated the cash-burning shell.

METC.valuation_basis: SoP of out-of-favor met-coal core (trough) + risk-weighted Brook Mine REE optionality. Met coal: normalized mid-cycle EBITDA ~$60M (FY25 Adj EBITDA $36.1M; Q1-26 Adj EBITDA -$1.8M trough; FY26 guide 3.7-4.1Mt prod at thin $16/t cash margin) at 4.5x small-cap met-coal comp = $270M EV. Brook Mine REE: Fluor PEA (Jul-2025) pre-tax NPV8 $1.197B; post-tax ~$0.88B, but MOUs (REalloys/Mulberry) are NON-BINDING and Phase-1 volumes are late-2026/2027, so risk-weighted at 0.25 readiness = $220M. Net CASH $304M (Q1-26 BS). 53.8M sh. Build = (270+220+304)/53.8 = $14.76. Floor strips ALL REE credit (coal-only de-rated): (270+304)/53.8 ~ $10.7, taken to ~$9.50 on a deeper coal trough.

sop_components.basis: Normalized mid-cycle EBITDA above FY25 Adj $36.1M trough / Q1-26 -$1.8M; 4.5x vs small-cap met-coal peers (CEIX/AMR/HCC trade ~3-5x). PR 2026-05-11 FY26 guide 3.7-4.1Mt, $95-100/t cash cost.

Fluor independent PEA 2025-07-02 pre-tax NPV8 $1.197B (post-tax ~$0.88B), initial capex $473M; 0.25 readiness/probability factor as REalloys+Mulberry+DOE/NETL ties are NON-BINDING MOUs and Phase-1 (525tpa NdPr) is late-2026/2027.

MIDD.valuation_basis: Sum-of-parts of CURRENT MIDD (what a holder owns through the Jul-6-2026 Midera/MFP 1:1 spin): RemainCo Commercial Foodservice + Midera Food Processing + 49% Composition Brands stake. CFS: 2026E Adj EBITDA $575M (mgmt Investor-Day, May-2026) at 12x pure-play commercial-foodservice = $6,900M. Midera (Food Processing, ticker MFP): 2026E Adj EBITDA $165M at 11x (JBT/GEA food-processing comps ~11-13x) = $1,815M. Composition Brands (ex-Residential Kitchen): 49% of the $885M Dec-2025 26North deal mark = $434M stake_mv. Net debt $1,697M (Q1-26, already reflects $540M Composition cash inflow; 2.3x lev). Seller note $135M = asset (-135 adj). 45.2M sh. Build = (6,900+1,815+434 -1,697 +135)/45.2 = $167.85.

sop_components.basis: 2026E Adj EBITDA $575M (Investor Day May-2026, ~23% margin); 12x pure-play commercial-foodservice (MIDD historical 12-13x; peers WELB/illuminate).
2026E Adj EBITDA $165M on $930M sales (~21% margin); 11x food-processing-equipment comps (JBT ~12x, GEA ~11x).
26North bought 51% Dec-2025 valuing the residential-kitchen business (Viking/AGA/Kamado Joe) at $885M; MIDD retained 49% non-controlling stake at that mark.

MRNA.valuation_basis: BARBELL on mRNA-1010 seasonal-flu regulatory event: FDA accepted the BLA with Aug-5-2026 PDUFA and scheduled a RARE VRBPAC adcomm Jun-18-2026 to publicly weigh safety/effectiveness. Unusual binary in that efficacy is largely established (Phase 3 beat comparator in older adults) — the swing factor is FDA political/regulatory risk under the revised vaccine-review approach, not the data. Flu itself is a low-margin/competitive indication; true value of a win is de-risking the broader respiratory franchise (RSV, combo, next-gen COVID) which faces the same FDA. ~$8B+ cash provides a floor; ~385M sh. Source: Moderna 8-K (exhibit991-02102026), FDA VRBPAC adcomm notice, Pharmaceutical Technology / PharmExec / Yahoo (adcomm).
MRNA.prob_basis: win_prob 0.55: approval is more-likely-than-not on strong Phase 3 data + filed BLA, but FDA convening a rare VRBPAC specifically to publicly weigh safety/effectiveness historically lowers approval odds vs a routine review and signals genuine scrutiny. EV barbell = 0.55*$58 + 0.45*$36 = $48.10 vs $47.12 ref (+2.1%); near-symmetric because flu upside is modest while a CRL/negative adcomm would de-rate the whole respiratory pipeline (FDA-hostility readthrough).

MUX.valuation_basis: SoP of gold/silver mining ops + 46.3% McEwen Copper stake (Los Azules), where the catalyst (Q4-2026 McEwen Copper ~$300M IPO + SocGen-led $4B project financing, mandate 2026-05-14) crystallizes the stake at a copper-rally-driven step-up. Gold ops: run-rate EBITDA ~$65M (Q4-25 net income $38.1M; Fox/Gold Bar/San Jose) at 5x junior-producer = $325M. McEwen Copper stake: LAST FINANCING implied MUX's 46.3% = $457M (=$987M total). Post-catalyst mark steps up to ~$1,588M total equity (heavily discounting Los Azules after-tax NPV $2.9B at $4.35/lb -> ~$6.3B at spot for Argentina/financing/minority risk); MUX 46.3% post-IPO ~$735M. Net CASH $52.7M. 59.7M sh. Build = (325+735+52.7)/59.7 = $18.64 -- IPO re-rate is largely IN the $18.39 price.

sop_components.basis: Run-rate ops EBITDA (Fox Complex, Gold Bar, 49% San Jose); Q4-25 net income $38.1M. 5x junior gold producer.

Catalyst-implied IPO mark: last financing put MUX's 46.3% at $457M ($987M total); stepped to ~$1,588M total reflecting copper rally (Los Azules after-tax NPV $2.9B@$4.35/lb, ~$6.3B@spot) net of Argentina/financing/IPO-minority discount. SocGen mandate 2026-05-14.

NAMS.valuation_basis: Barbell on obicetrapib (CETP inhibitor). LDL franchise already de-risked: BROADWAY/BROOKLYN/TANDEM Phase 3 positive (35-50% LDL-C lowering), EMA MAA VALIDATED/accepted (Menarini-filed), EMA/UK/Swiss decisions 2H26. The multi-billion binary is PREVAIL CVOT (>9,500 pts, MACE) -> interim 4Q26, results pushed to 1Q27. NAMS retains US economics (the prize); EU via Menarini royalties (low-double to mid-20s% + up to EUR863M milestones). Cash $707.3M (3/31/26), 116.9M sh (~$6/sh net cash). WIN (EU approval + supportive PREVAIL -> obicetrapib becomes a CV-outcomes statin-adjunct, 7MM peak ~$1.4B+, US-owned): re-rate to ~$60 (top of analyst range $37.77-$59.33; Street median $47.51; William Blair FV $43; one analyst 90% PoS, >100% upside). LOSE (PREVAIL fails to show CV benefit -> niche LDL-lowering only, value collapses toward cash + modest EU royalty stream): ~$14. win_prob 0.55 calibrated: near-term EU approval ~90% (drug works, MAA validated) but the price-defining CVOT leg is a true coin-flip-plus (CETP class has a graveyard, but obicetrapib has clean LDL + ApoB data and ongoing Alzheimer's biomarker signal). EV = 0.55*60 + 0.45*14 = ~$39, below spot $31 only if you weight downside heavier; at 0.55 EV>spot.
NAMS.prob_basis: Blended: ~90% near-term EU/UK/Swiss approval (Phase 3 LDL data positive + EMA MAA already validated) x ~0.6 conditional on the value-defining PREVAIL CVOT showing MACE benefit (CETP-class historical failures offset by obicetrapib's clean LDL/ApoB profile and Alzheimer's biomarker signal); one sell-side analyst assigns 90% PoS. Calibrated to 0.55 for the full re-rate.
NAMS.valuation_basis_source: NAMS Q1-2026 8-K (cash, milestones); EMA MAA acceptance PR (Menarini, 2025); GlobalData/MarketBeat peak-sales & PT 2026

NATL.valuation_basis: Brink's (BCO) acquires NCR Atleos: $30.00 cash + 0.1574 BCO/sh (definitive, DEFM14A). Mark-to-acquirer at BCO $101.16 -> stock leg 0.1574*101.16 = $15.92; total consideration = $45.92 (vs the $50.40 struck at BCO $129.58 on 25-Feb-2026 -> deal value fell with BCO -22%). NATL $44.13 -> gross spread $1.79 (~4.1%) to a Q1-2027 close (~7-9 mo), ~6% annualized. De-risked: HSR EARLY TERMINATION granted by FTC/DOJ; noteholder consent obtained; both special meetings 30-Jun-2026; only money-transmitter license transfers + shareholder votes remain. break_prob ~0.09. Undisturbed $40.80 (24% premium math off 25-Feb-2026 close). Note: a chunk of the 'spread' is beta to BCO via the stock leg, not pure deal risk.
NATL.valuation_basis_source: NATL DEFM14A / BCO S-4/A 2026; HSR early-termination & noteholder-consent PRs; BCO live quote $101.16

NEOG.valuation_basis: Catalyst = $160M Genomics/GeneSeek sale to Zoetis (announced 2026-03-02, closes 1H-FY27) to delever <3x. This is a SMALL deleveraging event (~$0.74/sh of net-debt reduction), NOT a re-rate. Post-catalyst value = Food Safety RemainCo on a cleaner balance sheet: FY26E total Adj EBITDA ~$175M LESS ~$15M Genomics = ~$160M RemainCo EBITDA at a de-rated 12x (serial post-3M-merger disappointer; just wrote off ~$1B goodwill) = $1,920M EV. Net debt: $900M gross (Q3-26) - $160M cash = $740M, pro-forma post-sale ~$580M. ~217.5M sh. Build = (1,920 - 580)/217.5 = $6.16 -- BELOW live $9.07. The $12 analyst PT embeds a multi-year FY28 recovery (~$107M EPS), not THIS catalyst. Honest NO_UPSIDE.

sop_components.basis: FY26E Adj EBITDA ~$175M (mgmt) less ~$15M Genomics; Q3-26 margin 22.8%. 12x de-rated vs diagnostics/food-safety peers (10-14x) given integration/Petrifilm execution risk and ~3.3x pro-forma leverage.

NPAC.valuation_basis: SPAC cash-box arb. NPAC (def. BCA 16-Mar-2026) takes Abra Financial (digital-asset wealth platform) public at $750M pre-money as ABRX on Nasdaq; $40M minimum-net-cash close condition; Abra holders to own majority. IPO put $10.05/unit in trust (Apr-2025); pro-rata redemption ~$10.33 at 12/31/25, accreting ~5%/yr -> ~$10.45 by mid-2026. NPAC trades $10.37 ~ trust. Protection = redemption right: a holder at/below trust gets ~$10.40 back if the deal breaks or they redeem, so 'break' is not a loss -> fair_value_target set just above trust ($10.60) for deal-completion optionality on a speculative crypto de-SPAC (these rarely sustain premiums post-close); downside_floor = redemption value $10.40. Asymmetric but tiny absolute spread; the warrant (1/3, $11.50 strike) is the free option, not modeled in equity floor.
NPAC.valuation_basis_source: NPAC 8-K/425 16-Mar-2026 (Abra $750M, $40M min cash); NPAC 10-K (trust $10.05, redemption $10.33 @12/31/25); IPO PR Apr-2025

NRIX.valuation_basis: EHA2026 oral on Jun-14-26 is an INCREMENTAL same-trial Phase 1a/b CLL update for bexobrutideg (NX-5948), NOT an approval binary, so this is a soft event-weighted re-rate, not a coin-flip. Anchor: ASH Dec-2025 showed ~81% ORR (2 CRs) in heavily pretreated R/R CLL, median PFS 22.1m; Roche validated mechanism with $700M upfront / up to $2.3B deal (clinicaltrialvanguard, 2025). Sell-side targets cluster $30-36 (Morgan Stanley OW $36, Stifel $33, HCW $31, Mizuho $30; investing.com 2026). WIN (good/confirmatory RP2D data) re-rates toward lower-half of that range with a haircut for incremental nature -> target_on_win $24. LOSS (in-line/soft print) drifts back to pre-run support ~$11 (NOT cash $3.7/sh: co. is well-funded $320M and Roche-backed, so floor is trading support not liquidation). EV%: 0.45*(24/15.61-1)+0.55*(11/15.61-1)=+0.080.
NRIX.prob_basis: Incremental same-trial confirmatory readout (not a registrational binary); calibrated ~0.45 that updated EHA data is a clear positive surprise vs in-line. Mechanism already de-risked by ASH-2025 efficacy + Roche $2.3B partnership; risk is the print is merely confirmatory and the run-up (yearLow 8.20 -> 15.61) unwinds.

NUVL.valuation_basis: Two accepted NDAs are high-probability regulatory follow-through, not a coin-flip. zidesamtinib (ROS1+ NSCLC) PDUFA 9/18/26; neladalkib (ALK+ NSCLC) PDUFA 11/27/26 under PRIORITY REVIEW (investors.nuvalent.com 5/27/26 + FDA acceptance, investing.com). ~78.88M sh (73.45M Cl-A + 5.44M Cl-B), $1.3B cash ($159.7M + $1.128B mkt sec, 3/31/26 10-Q) ~$16.5/sh, runway into 2029. WIN (dual approval) re-rates back to/above prior highs on best-in-class ROS1/ALK launch optionality -> target_on_win $115 (vs 52w-high $113). LOSS (a CRL/delay) pulls to pipeline+cash floor ~$55 (cash ~$16.5/sh + deep wholly-owned pipeline option value). EV%: 0.80*(115/87.91-1)+0.20*(55/87.91-1)=+0.172.
NUVL.prob_basis: Both NDAs ACCEPTED (neladalkib on Priority Review) on strong pivotal data in a high-unmet-need TKI-pretreated setting; approval base rate for accepted oncology NDAs with breakthrough-caliber data is high -> 0.80. Downside is regulatory (CMC/label/CRL) not efficacy.

NWE.valuation_basis: Fixed 0.98-ratio all-stock merger-of-equals with Black Hills (BKH). deal_price = 0.98 x BKH live $72.29 = $70.84/sh (stock deal: consideration floats with acquirer). undisturbed_price $54.92 = NWE close 8/18/25 (implied $59.14 deal value at 7.67% premium / 1.0767; insidearbitrage 8/2025, BKH/NWE 8-K). Major gates FIRED: FERC approved 5/29/26, HSR cleared 4/20/26, both shareholder votes passed Apr-26; remaining = MT/SD/NE state PUC approvals, close H2-26. Heavily de-risked -> break_prob 0.08. Spread at ref $69.89: (70.84-69.89)/(69.89-54.92)=0.063, thin as expected for a late-stage cleared MoE; principal risk is a state-commission condition, not collapse.

PHVS.valuation_basis: The one real forward binary is CHAPTER-3 (deucrictibant XR) Phase 3 prophylaxis topline in 3Q2026. The 1H2026 on-demand NDA (off positive RAPIDe-3, Dec-2025) is regulatory follow-through, not a binary, and de-risks the molecule/PK but prophylaxis is a different, harder bar (B2-antagonist chronic-prophylaxis mechanism is the weak link). 69.88M sh, ~EUR247M (~$265M) cash 3/31/26 (~$3.8/sh), runway to 2028 after $132M raise. WIN (prophylaxis success) opens the larger chronic-prophylaxis market vs Takhzyro/Orladeyo -> re-rate to ~$45 (vs 52w-high $33.33, reflecting expanded TAM). LOSS retains an approvable on-demand franchise, so floor ~$22 sits well above cash. EV%: 0.55*(45/31.48-1)+0.45*(22/31.48-1)=+0.101.
PHVS.prob_basis: Calibrated 0.55: molecule de-risked on PK/efficacy by positive RAPIDe-3 on-demand Phase 3, but oral B2-antagonist chronic prophylaxis is a distinct, tougher endpoint with weaker mechanistic read-across; modest edge above coin-flip.

PVLA.valuation_basis: CAUTION - the flagged de-risking binary ALREADY FIRED: Phase 3 SELVA topline was POSITIVE 2/24/26 (met primary + key secondary, mean +2.13 on ML-MM IGA; ir.palvellatx.com). Forward path is NDA follow-through, not a binary: 505(b)(2) NDA in 2H2026 -> potential approval/launch 1H2027 for QTORIN rapamycin in microcystic LMs (first-to-market, no approved therapy, BTD/Orphan/Fast Track). 15.74M sh, $261.9M cash 3/31/26 (~$16.6/sh) funds launch. WIN (clean NDA->approval, launch confirmation) re-rates toward sell-side $220-250 (Stifel $250, Stephens $220) with a haircut to $200. LOSS (CMC/label/timing slip; data already positive) pulls to ~$70, well above cash given de-risked pivotal. EV%: 0.75*(200/106.24-1)+0.25*(70/106.24-1)=+0.577.
PVLA.prob_basis: 0.75 reflects that the hard scientific binary (Phase 3) is already won; residual risk is execution/regulatory (505(b)(2) filing, CMC, label, approval timing) for a first-in-class orphan with BTD/FT - historically high approval odds once pivotal is positive. NOT a fresh coin-flip.

RYAM.valuation_basis: Standalone de-rated SoP. My OWN SoP on conservative normalized EBITDA: High Purity Cellulose (Cellulose Specialties + Biomaterials + commodity) normalized ~$150M (Q1-26 HPC Adj EBITDA $24M; FY25 total ~$135-140M; mgmt >$300M-by-2027 target heavily discounted as RYAM has missed EVERY guide) at 6.5x (Borregaard/Bracell/Sappi cellulose comps, de-rated for leverage+execution) = $975M. Paperboard+High-Yield Pulp normalized ~$20M (Q1-26 -$5M, new third-party supply) at 5x = $100M. Net debt $695M (Q1-26, 4.3x net-secured lev). Pension/environmental/OPEB drag +$120M (signed, subtracted). 67.4M sh. Build = (975+100 -695 -120)/67.4 = $3.86. Live $7.99 prices takeout hope ABOVE standalone SoP.

sop_components.basis: Normalized ~$150M (Q1-26 segment Adj EBITDA $24M annualizes ~$96M; FY25 total ~$135-140M; 2027 >$300M target discounted). 6.5x de-rated cellulose-specialties (Borregaard ~9x, Bracell/Sappi lower) for leverage+serial-miss.
Normalized ~$20M (Q1-26 -$5M on new third-party paperboard supply + Asian HYP oversupply); 5x commodity-pulp multiple.

STOK.valuation_basis: Bio-convergence BARBELL on zorevunersen (STK-001) Ph3 EMPEROR in Dravet syndrome. Per 11-Jan-2026 BusinessWire timeline update: enrollment completion pushed to Q2-2026, value-inflection Ph3 topline to mid-2027, rolling NDA H1-2027; $411M cash (yr-end 2025) funds ops into 2028, fully covering the trial (investor.stoketherapeutics.com). Q1-2026 cash+investments $343.5M, net cash -$152M (10-Q) => ~$5.5/sh cash on ~62.3M sh. win_prob 0.60 CALIBRATED: registrational Ph3 in a CNS/ASO indication with strong prior Ph1/2a + OLE seizure-reduction & cognition data, Breakthrough Therapy Designation, and Biogen as ex-US partner -> upper end of the ~50-60% neurology-Ph3->approval base rate. target_on_win $62 ~ Guggenheim $60 Buy PT on ~$2.5B peak-sales (investing.com); downside_on_loss $7 ~ cash floor (~$5.5/sh) + residual TANGO/ASO platform. EV = 0.60*62 + 0.40*7 = ~$40.0 vs ref $29.13 (+37%).
STOK.prob_basis: Ph3 registrational in Dravet with positive Ph1/2a+OLE efficacy (durable seizure reduction + cognition/behavior gains on top of SoC), FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation, and Biogen ex-US partnership -> 0.60, upper end of the ~50-60% neurology Ph3->approval base rate. Topline mid-2027 (single sham-controlled study, 50/50 randomization, ~150 pts).

TBN.valuation_basis: Override of supply_timing lane: TBN is a risked-NAV re-rating on Beetaloo first gas (Q3-2026, binding 40 TJ/day GSA with NT Government, Sturt Plateau Compression Facility 88% complete & on-budget at end-Apr-2026). Valued as risked Phase-1 producing NAV + Beetaloo appraisal/LNG optionality across ~2.9M net acres, net of slight net cash and $151M minority (Beetaloo EP JVs). Beetaloo precedents value de-risked parcels at $2,900-5,500/acre vs ~$400/acre implied in EV; I credit ~$300/acre blended risked across the full 2.9M acres given pre-appraisal/capex intensity. Shares set to live mkt-cap/price (974.1/34.40=28.3M) for entity consistency.

sop_components.segment: Sturt Plateau Phase-1 producing asset (first gas Q3-2026)
sop_components.basis: Binding 40 TJ/day GSA with NT Government + SPCF 88% complete on-budget (Apr-2026); risked NAV of initial wells/facility + basin proof-of-concept premium

sop_components.segment: Beetaloo appraisal + NT LNG optionality (~2.9M net acres)
sop_components.basis: Beetaloo precedent $2,900-5,500/acre on de-risked parcels (e.g. Daly Waters farm-out); risked to ~$300/acre blended across 2.9M acres = ~$870M, haircut to $600M for development-capex intensity

TBRG.valuation_basis: All-cash merger: IKS Health (Inventurus Knowledge Solutions) to acquire TruBridge for $26.25/sh cash, EV ~$557M. Definitive agreement 4/23/2026; DEFM14A filed; special meeting 7/7/2026 8:00am CT; closing conditions = TBRG shareholder vote + HSR waiting period; expected close Q3-2026 (BusinessWire/StockTitan, 4/23/2026; SEC DEFM14A). ~27% of votes locked via support agreements (L6 Holdings, Pinetree Capital, Ocho). EV bridge check: equity 26.25x15.0M=$394M + net debt $133M ≈ $527M, +~$30M options/leakage ~ $557M EV, internally consistent. break_prob low: friendly, fully-financed, supported, immaterial antitrust (rural-healthcare IT/services). fair_value=deal_price 26.25; floor=pre-bid trough ~$13.88 (52wk low ≈ undisturbed level). reference_rr=(26.25-26.05)/(26.05-13.88)=0.016 — only +0.8% gross to close but ~4-8wk horizon (high annualized), asymmetric ~47% drop on a break.
TBRG.prob_basis: Friendly all-cash deal, 27% voting support locked, financed, HSR-only regulatory (no overlap); small-cap healthcare-IT, no competing bid risk -> low break ~8%

TECK.valuation_basis: Stock merger of equals: each Teck share -> 1.3301 Anglo American (AAL) ordinary shares (Teck/Anglo press releases 9/9/2025; teck.com/merger). All-share, so deal value = current acquirer price x ratio. AAL ordinary value (USD) = NGLOY ADR $25.955 x 2 (ADR=0.5 ordinary, per Anglo IR/SEC F-6) = $51.91; x 1.3301 = $69.05 implied per Teck share. Approvals: Canada (ICA), South Korea cleared; ONLY China MOFCOM outstanding (MLex 2026); close targeted Sep-2026 to Mar-2027 (mining.com, Anglo guidance). No-premium MoE (post AAL $4.5B special dividend, effective premium ~1%) -> minimal divestiture/antitrust risk; principal risk = China geopolitical/timing. fair_value=deal_price $69.05; floor=undisturbed Teck NYSE close $34.31 (9/5/2025, last pre-announcement; 9/9 gapped to $39.07) — but floor is SOFT: Teck's standalone copper value has re-rated to ~$62 on commodity strength, so a break would not retrace to $34. reference_rr=(69.05-62.535)/(62.535-34.31)=0.23 (gross spread ~10.4% to a 3-9mo close).
TECK.prob_basis: MoE board+shareholder approved; Canada+S.Korea cleared, only China MOFCOM left; no-premium structure limits antitrust remedy risk but China is a real geopolitical wildcard -> break ~15%

TEVA.valuation_basis: BARBELL on a single pipeline catalyst, NOT cash (Teva is a $39B going concern; this is one swing factor on a large-cap, not a cash-shell binary). Asset: TEV-'749 once-monthly subcutaneous olanzapine LAI (with Medincell) for schizophrenia; NDA accepted by FDA 2/20/2026 (standard 10-mo review) -> PDUFA Q4-2026; differentiated no-post-injection-monitoring (no PDSS) label; EMA MAA also accepted 5/21/2026 (Teva IR; GlobeNewswire). Long-acting schizophrenia franchise (Uzedy + TEV-'749) peak sales guided $1.5-2.0B (Teva). win_prob 0.80: accepted NDA, Ph3 SOLARIS met endpoints, clean clinical differentiation; chief risk = injection-site/PDSS safety language at label. target_on_win ~$36: ~$1.75B peak x ~2x risk-adj NPV multiple ~ +$3-4B value over ~0.9B ADR-equiv shares ~ +$2.5/sh re-rate. downside_on_loss ~$31: CRL/label-restriction trims the LAI option but base generics/innovative business intact (floor is the franchise, not cash). Expected value (EV%): 0.80x(+7.5%)+0.20x(-7.4%) ~ +4.5% vs ref $33.48.
TEVA.prob_basis: Accepted NDA (2/20/2026) + positive Ph3 (SOLARIS) + EMA MAA accepted; differentiated no-monitoring label; residual risk is post-injection-safety labeling -> calibrated approval prob ~0.80. Large-cap overlay: catalyst is incremental, not cash-binary.

TLX.valuation_basis: Forward binary on Pixclara, but OVERLAY on a real commercial radiopharma (NOT a cash-shell): Telix TTM revenue ~AUD 1.21B from Illuccix, ~338M shares, near-breakeven (FMP Q4-2025). Asset: TLX101-Px (Pixclara, 18F-FET) PET imaging for recurrent/progressive glioma; NDA resubmitted 3/13/2026, FDA accepted ~4/9/2026, PDUFA goal date 9/11/2026; Orphan + Fast Track; 18F-FET already in NCCN guidelines. Prior Zircaix CRL/issue was CMC-only (Form 483 at two third-party sites), not efficacy/safety -> de-risks Pixclara clinical bar but CMC re-inspection risk lingers. NO Pixclara revenue in FY26 guidance -> approval is incremental, not transformative. win_prob 0.75: CMC-only history + guideline-backed agent, offset by manufacturing-inspection risk. target_on_win ~$10.0 (modest sentiment/pipeline re-rate). downside_on_loss ~$8.3: a CRL dents sentiment but the Illuccix commercial base + Zircaix optionality are the floor (business, not cash). Ref $9.28 ADR. EV%: 0.75x(+7.8%)+0.25x(-10.6%) ~ +3.2%.
TLX.prob_basis: Pixclara PDUFA 9/11/2026; resubmission accepted 4/2026; Orphan+Fast Track; prior issue was CMC at 3rd-party sites (not efficacy) -> ~0.75 approval. Commercial-co overlay: small incremental asset on a ~AUD1.2B Illuccix base, so a CRL is a sentiment hit, not a wipeout.

UROY.valuation_basis: OVERRIDE of clean merger-arb: this is a TRANSFORMATIVE REVERSE-STYLE COMBINATION, not a UROY buyout (triage flag confirmed). UROY to combine with Sweetwater Royalties (92% from Orion + Ontario Teachers') into newly-formed 'New URC', NASDAQ-listed; arrangement agreement 4/16/2026; shareholder vote ~July-2026 requiring 66-2/3% of URC votes (SEC 6-K; StockTitan). Consideration to sellers: $330M cash + $813M stock issued at $3.64/sh New URC. Sweetwater EV ~$1.9B incl ~$625M debt -> combined entity materially MORE LEVERED; existing URC holders end at only ~41% pro-forma (Orion 43%, OTPP 16%) -> NOT accretive cash-out; holders roll into a larger, geared vehicle. NO fixed cash consideration to arb -> this is a pro-forma NAV re-rate. Anchor: struck New URC share value $3.64 (best 'deal' reference). KEY TELL: stock FELL on announcement (undisturbed 4/15/2026 close $3.92 -> $3.76 -> $3.63, now $3.205), i.e. the market is pricing dilution/leverage/execution risk; current price sits BELOW both the strike and the undisturbed level. fair_value_target=$3.64 (struck pro-forma share value); downside_floor=$2.90 (deal-fail/uranium-soft haircut toward 52wk-low $2.20 zone). NOTE undisturbed=$3.92 sits ABOVE the strike -> negative information content; reference_rr=(3.64-3.205)/(3.205-2.90)=1.43 is thin and overhung by dilution.
UROY.prob_basis: Needs 66-2/3% URC shareholder vote; existing holders only ~41% pro-forma and stock trading below the $3.64 strike signals ambivalence toward a more-levered reverse combination -> elevated break/renegotiation risk ~25%

VFS.valuation_basis: Distressed deleveraging recovery. VinFast restructuring (6-K 12-13 May 2026; Bloomberg 16 May): sells VND13.3T (~$530M) Vietnam manufacturing entity VFTP to founder-led Future Investment R&D group, transferring ~VND182T (~$6.9B) of debt/obligations OFF the Singapore listco and extinguishing a ~$404.8M P-Note to Vingroup/VIG. Listco (renamed VFVN) keeps global IP, brand, R&D, after-sales and overseas subs (Australia/Germany) and becomes ESSENTIALLY DEBT-FREE; production continues under a VFTP supply agreement. EGM passed 27 May 2026; creditor approvals pending, close targeted Q3-2026. Net-debt bridge: pre-deal net debt ~VND98.9T (~$3.96B at ~25k VND/USD) -> ~$0 post. Recovery value = asset-light, debt-free EV brand re-rated modestly off the deleveraging; ~2.34B sh (mktcap $7.23B/$3.09). NO CLEAN COMP for a debt-free brand-only EV listco selling its only plants to its own controlling shareholder (related-party leakage risk caps upside), so floor kept tight at the 52wk-low / current pre-close level $2.80. recovery_value $4.25 (~+38% on deleveraging re-rate).
VFS.instrument_ref: VFS common (NASDAQ); listco = VinFast Auto Ltd (Singapore), post-deal VFVN

VIR.valuation_basis: Single clinical binary, BARBELL not ratio. ECLIPSE 1 Phase 3 (tobevibart+elebsiran in chronic hepatitis delta), fully enrolled 03-Nov-2025, NOT fired. Latest company guidance (Jan & May-2026 updates): primary completion Q4-2026, TOPLINE Q1-2027 (context's 'Q4 2026' is the endpoint-completion date; readout slips to Q1-2027). win_prob 0.58 CALIBRATED: SOLSTICE Phase 2 (public/priced, EASL May-2026) showed 88% undetectable HDV RNA at 96wks for the combo vs 46% antibody-mono -> strong target-engagement de-risking, well-defined antiviral MoA, but Ph3 composite virologic+ALT endpoint is a higher bar (haircut from ~0.7 to 0.58). downside_on_loss $5.50 = cash floor + residual oncology option: Q1-26 BS cash+ST+LT investments ~$805M (~$4.76/sh on ~169M sh, net cash $189M) PLUS de-risked Astellas oncology (VIR-5500 PSMA T-cell-engager, $335M upfront/near-term + 50/50 US profit split + ex-US royalties) that survives an HDV miss. target_on_win $20 = consensus 12-mo PT ($17-30 range; mean ~$20.6) anchored to HDV approval optionality. Prob-weighted EV = 0.58*$20 + 0.42*$5.50 = $13.91 vs ref $8.49 (+64%). reference_rr 5.3 = prob-weighted reward/risk: 0.58*(20-8.49) / 0.42*(8.49-5.50).
VIR.prob_basis: SOLSTICE Ph2 88% vs 46% undetectable HDV RNA @96wks (EASL May-2026) = strong target engagement; haircut for harder Ph3 composite endpoint -> 0.58

VLRS.valuation_basis: Stock-for-stock MERGER OF EQUALS (override of pure cash-arb framing): Viva Aerobus merges INTO Volaris (VLRS survives) to form Grupo Mas Vuelos; Viva holders get ~1.079B newly-issued VLRS shares (~$1.08B), each side ~50% pro-forma. NO per-share cash paid to VLRS holders, so the catalyst value is the SYNERGY RE-RATE of the surviving listco, not a takeout premium. Shareholders approved 25-Mar-2026 (91.8% of capital, 93.7% quorum; 6-K, FlightGlobal). Standalone (Q1-26): ~115M sh, net debt $3.08B (mostly aircraft leases), lumpy TTM EBITDA ~$175M (Q4'25 +$114.5M skews it). Combined carries ~69% of Mexican domestic pax (duopoly ULCC) -> network/cost synergies lift pro-forma EBITDA toward ~$500-550M; at a conservative 4.5x EV/EBITDA (Latam ULCC comp; thin clean comps post-Aeromexico) the synergy accretion to legacy VLRS holders supports a re-rate toward the 52wk-high ~$10.80. deal_price (=pro-forma fair value per legacy VLRS sh) $10.50; undisturbed_price $5.50 = pre-announcement (Dec-2025) level (52wk-low $4.15). break_prob 0.30: cross-border antitrust on Mexico's two largest ULCCs combining (DLA Piper flagged COFECE/regulatory review) is the live risk despite shareholder approval. reference_rr 1.61 = (10.50-7.415)/(7.415-5.50).

WBS.valuation_basis: Clean late-stage cash+stock arb. Banco Santander (SAN) acquires Webster: $48.75 CASH + 2.0548 SAN ADS per WBS share (definitive agreement 03-Feb-2026; $12.3B; DEFM14A). Two-step: VA reincorporation merger then statutory share exchange. WBS shareholder vote PASSED 26-May-2026 (already fired). FLOATING deal value at live acquirer price: $48.75 + 2.0548 x SAN $12.195 = $73.81 (vs the ~$75.6 headline struck at SAN's higher Feb level). Remaining gate = US bank regulatory clearance (Fed/OCC) for a foreign acquirer; SAN retains the right not to close on a 'materially burdensome regulatory condition.' Close guided H2-2026. break_prob 0.12: signed definitive, vote done, well-capitalized strategic buyer funding ~$7.9B cash on hand; modest regulatory + SAN-ADS market risk. fair_value_target = floating deal value $73.81; downside_floor = undisturbed $65 (Feb-2 close area; deal = 16% premium to 10-day VWAP ~$65.16, 9% over all-time-high). reference_rr 0.19 = (73.81-72.415)/(72.415-65) -- thin residual spread, correct for a post-vote arb mostly carrying regulatory tail + the floating SAN leg.

XOMA.valuation_basis: Cash + CVR arb where the CVR is the swing. Ligand (LGND) acquires XOMA Royalty: $39.00 CASH + one non-transferable CVR per share (signed definitive 27-Apr-2026; ~$739M; 8-K/DEFA14A). CVR = a portion of 75% of net proceeds from XOMA's pending Janssen litigation. Holdco reorg + CVR spin; conditions = antitrust + majority approval; $40M break fee. Stock at $42.28 trades ~$3.28 ABOVE the $39 cash floor -> market already ascribes ~$3.28 to the CVR. fair_value_target $42.50 = $39.00 cash + ~$3.50 risk-adjusted CVR (modestly above the live market-implied CVR for litigation upside). undisturbed_price $34.21 = 30-day VWAP as of 24-Apr-2026 (deal cash = 14% premium to it). break_prob 0.10: signed definitive royalty-aggregator tuck-in, low antitrust risk, majority approval pending; deal cash alone exceeds undisturbed by ~14% so downside is well-supported. reference_rr 0.03 = (42.50-42.28)/(42.28-34.21) -- de-minimis residual to cash+market-CVR; the real optionality is CVR realization above the implied mark, not deal-spread capture.

CCOI.valuation_basis: Distressed, heavily-levered equity (forced asset sales to deleverage; dividend SLASHED to $0.02/qtr Q2-2026 from ~$1.005, confirming distress). Total debt incl capital leases $2.66B vs cash $140M -> net debt $2.52B; BOOK EQUITY NEGATIVE (-$104M); mkt cap only $840M (50.08M sh). Recovery = SoP of the enterprise minus debt: network/transit EBITDA ex-IPv4/T-Mobile ~$108M x 6x = $648M; IPv4 portfolio (37.8M addresses owned, ~15M leased; mkt value $570-760M, ~$370M already securitized into the debt) credited at a generous mid $1,300M reflecting scarcity/$2B bull marks; remaining ~44 data centers ~$600M (10 best sold to I Squared for $225M on ~Jun-12 close); T-Mobile $700M IP-transit contract residual PV ~$400M. EV ~$2,948M - net debt $2,522M = ~$426M residual equity. CATALYST (the $225M DC asset sale) FIRED May-26; at live $16.78 the equity prices the IPv4 BULL case and reads NO_UPSIDE on conservative recovery marks -- it is a levered option on IPv4 re-rating, not a margin-of-safety long.
CCOI.instrument_ref: CCOI common equity (residual after $2.66B total debt incl. capital leases)

KTB.valuation_basis: Post-Lee SoP of a focused Wrangler + Helly Hansen apparel co. Catalyst: definitive sale of Lee to Authentic Brands for up to $1B ($750M upfront + $250M earnout, 2H-2026 close) + $750M buyback + deleveraging. Continuing-ops = Wrangler (FY25 sales $1.91B, segment op profit ~$415M, EBITDA ~$435M) at 9.5x and Helly Hansen (~$680M run-rate sales, ~$90M EBITDA) at 11x premium-outdoor multiple. Net debt $1.22B (Q1-2026); Lee $750M upfront cash credited as a -$750M adjustment (cash inflow adds to equity). FV $84 vs live $72.25 = modest upside; the $250M Lee earnout is excluded (upside-only).

sop_components.basis: Stable cash-generative heritage denim; 9.5x EBITDA vs Levi Strauss/VF mid-cap apparel ~8-10x
~$680M run-rate sales, ~$90M adj EBITDA; 11x for premium-outdoor growth vs Columbia/Amer Sports ~10-12x

CYTK.valuation_basis: BARBELL on the MAPLE-HCM sNDA, PDUFA 11/14/2026 (FDA accepted, standard review; CYTK PR/8-K). KEY: aficamten/MYQORZO is ALREADY APPROVED & LAUNCHED (US live ~680 starts Q1'26; EU/Germany 6/1/26), so this is a LABEL-EXPANSION (monotherapy vs metoprolol head-to-head), NOT a cash-vs-approval binary — a miss does NOT zero the drug, it keeps selling. win_prob 0.83 (label expansion on an already-approved molecule with POSITIVE MAPLE-HCM topline; prob_basis below). target_on_win $85: first-line monotherapy label accelerates the launch + ACACIA-HCM nHCM optionality, re-rate toward 52wk high $80.2 and above. downside_on_loss $56: lose the monotherapy label premium but drug still approved/commercial (liquidity ~$1,074M cash+investments offsets ~$1,041M converts, so floor is the base launch trajectory, NOT cash). EV = 0.83x85 + 0.17x56 = $80.07 vs ref $68.96 = +16.1% expected return. Asymmetric to the UPSIDE with capped downside since approval already in hand.
CYTK.prob_basis: Label-expansion sNDA on an already-FDA-approved drug; MAPLE-HCM met its endpoints (monotherapy vs metoprolol), standard review with 11/14/26 PDUFA; label-expansions on approved molecules with positive Ph3 historically clear ~80-85%

ALVO.valuation_basis: BARBELL on the AVT05 (Simponi/golimumab biosimilar) + AVT06 (Eylea/aflibercept biosimilar) BLA RESUBMISSION (resubmitted 6/4/26, globenewswire/ALVO 6-K; FDA ~6-month review -> decision ~Dec-2026; Teva commercializes). This is a RESUBMISSION after a MANUFACTURING (Reykjavik facility/PAAL) CRL, not an efficacy question, with reinspection completed May-2026 — but it FAILS the deep-asymmetry bar (repeat facility risk, highly levered). win_prob 0.65 (manufacturing-CRL resubmissions post-reinspection clear ~70% historically, discounted for the facility's repeat observations). target_on_win $6.00: dual approval of two high-value biosimilars (Eylea is a ~$9bn reference brand) with Teva re-rates toward the $5.39 200d-avg with launch optionality. downside_on_loss $2.75: another CRL on a company with $1,393M net debt vs only $64M cash -> refinancing/dilution risk, NOT a clean cash floor, equity impaired toward 52wk low $2.94. EV = 0.65x6.00 + 0.35x2.75 = $4.86 vs ref $4.06 = +19.8%; CAVEAT ref is elevated/volatile (stock +14.5% today on the 6/4 resubmission pop). No clean comp on facility-pass odds -> floor widened.
ALVO.prob_basis: Manufacturing/facility-CRL resubmission (Reykjavik PAAL) with reinspection completed May-2026; such resubmissions historically approve ~70% once the site passes, discounted to 0.65 for repeat cGMP observations and refinancing fragility

BAK.valuation_basis: Mandatory tender offer (OPA, art. 254-A / CVM Res. 215) that Shine I FIP (IG4-advised, Vortx) must launch for ALL outstanding ON + Class-A PNA shares after acquiring 50.11% of voting / 34.32% of total capital from Novonor/NSP (closed Jun-2026; BAK 6-Ks; EGM 6/8/26). FAILS THE STRICT EDGE BAR: (1) NO per-share OPA price is disclosed — registration is pending CVM and carries a RESOLUTIVE CONDITION (if CVM definitively rejects the OPA the whole deal can unwind); (2) the control stake was paid in NSP DEBENTURES (R$20bn / ~US$3.5-3.7bn of distressed bank credit at face, 3 debentures per share), NOT cash, so there is NO clean cash comp for the minority OPA — per mandate the floor is WIDENED; (3) Petrobras already WAIVED its tag-along. BAK ADR = 2 PNA shares; PNA tag-along economics under 254-A are uncertain and the OPA will likely be struck off a depressed weighted-avg reference, not the control premium. fair_value_target $4.50 = a MODEST re-rate to a plausible OPA/weighted-avg exit (announced_return $4.50 + residual $0, OPA is a full cash-out, no stub). downside_floor $2.60: CVM rejection unwinds the deal -> standalone distressed petrochemical at cycle-trough margins, 52wk low $2.32. reference_rr = (4.50-3.53)/(3.53-2.60) = 1.04 — thin, consistent with the failed edge bar.
BAK.prob_basis: OPA price undefined and pending CVM registration with a resolutive (deal-unwind) condition; control consideration paid in distressed NSP debentures not cash, so no clean cash comp — floor widened per mandate

BORR.valuation_basis: Refinancing/distressed thesis FIRED: on 27-May-2026 BORR priced $2.035B senior secured notes ($1,100M 8.750% due 2032 + $935M 9.000% due 2034, settle ~10-Jun-2026) refinancing $1,128.1M of 10.000% 2028s + $770.7M of 10.375% 2030s; the $300M 3.50% convert due 2033 priced 15-Apr-2026 (conv ~$8.00). Net effect: maturity wall pushed 2028/30 -> 2032/34, secured coupon cut ~130bps on ~$1.9B (~$25M/yr interest saved), gross debt ~flat-to-+$0.1B. BORR is a going concern (not bankrupt), so 'recovery' = equity-on-EV. Q1-2026 was soft (rev $247M -5% QoQ, Adj EBITDA $88.5M, -$29M net loss, tender delays from ME conflict) but 2026 coverage 71% at ~$137k/day, Dayrate-Equiv Backlog $1.17B, fleet 34 rigs post-Noble. recovery_value $5.50 = ~5.5x normalized mid-cycle Adj EBITDA ~$600M = $3.3B EV less post-refi net debt ~$2.2B = ~$1.1B / ~316M dil sh, i.e. a modest re-rate on de-risking + lagged dayrate recovery (mgmt guides recovery lags oil 6-12mo). Floor $2.50 = cyclical-trough equity if dayrates stay soft (5x on ~$475M trough EBITDA leaves only ~$0.55/sh deep-trough; $2.50 reflects refi-de-risked low-end trading, vs 52wk low $1.79). Sources: PRNewswire 27-May-2026 notes pricing; PRNewswire 17-Apr-2026 convert; Stocktitan/energy-pedia Q1-2026 results.
BORR.instrument_ref: BORR common equity (post-refi); secured notes 8.750% 2032 / 9.000% 2034 + 3.50% conv 2033

CCRN.valuation_basis: Knox Lane all-cash take-private at $13.25/sh, $437M EV, signed 6-May-2026 (def. agreement). 31% premium to 6-May undisturbed close $10.11 / 45% to 90-day VWAP. HSR waiting period expires 22-Jun-2026 absent extension; expected close Q3-2026. Reciprocal $14.2M break fees (company termination & parent regulatory termination). PE-to-staffing buyer => negligible antitrust overlap; majority-stockholder vote pending but no overhang flagged. break_prob 0.08. fair_value_target=deal_price $13.25; downside_floor=undisturbed $10.11. At $13.195 gross spread is only $0.055 (0.4%) -> effectively arbed flat with ~1mo to HSR; thin remaining edge, asymmetric on a break. Sources: BusinessWire 6-May-2026; Kirkland & Ellis press release; CCRN 8-K/PREM14A (Stocktitan).
CCRN.prob_basis: Signed definitive all-cash deal, PE buyer (no antitrust overlap), HSR expiry 22-Jun-2026, reciprocal $14.2M break fees, only stockholder vote + customary conditions remain.

CEPT.valuation_basis: Cantor Equity Partners II de-SPAC of Securitize (RWA-tokenization leader); ticker -> SECZ. S-4 effective 5-Jun-2026, special meeting 29-Jun-2026, well inside 5-May-2027 liquidation deadline. Trust ~$248.8M -> illustrative redemption ~$10.51/public sh (HARD FLOOR if you redeem). $225M fully-committed PIPE at $10.00 (22.5M sh); NO public/sponsor warrants. Securitize valued at $1.25B equity (~125M pro-forma sh): 2026E revenue ~$110M (~11x sales), 2026E NI ~$32M (~39x), Q1-2026 rev $19.5M +39% YoY, tokenized AUM $3.4B / $25B AUA, BlackRock BUIDL anchor. This is a de-SPAC trading +20% over trust on RWA hype, NOT a fixed-cash deal: floor = trust/redemption $10.51, ceiling = where SECZ trades post-close. fair_value_target $13.50 = modest sustained premium-to-NAV (~$1.35B implied) for the category leader + BlackRock optionality; risk is post-close de-rate back toward NAV. break_prob 0.10 (committed PIPE, effective S-4, dated vote). Sources: Securitize 424B3 / CEPT DEFM14A & 425 (SEC/Stocktitan); PRNewswire 27-Oct-2025; The Block / news.bitcoin.com Q1-2026.

RCUS.prob_basis: 0.55 calibrated: prior ARC-20 cohort 46% cORR + class validation (belzutifan) de-risk the signal; discounted for small single-arm N~45, harder IO-experienced setting, and that a Ph1b read-through de-risks but does not equal the PEAK-1 Ph3 PFS endpoint

RIG.valuation_basis: RIG is the ACQUIRER in the all-stock Valaris merger (15.235 RIG/VAL, signed 9-Feb-2026, ~$5.8B equity / ~$17B combined EV at 6-Feb prices; TRT holders own ~53%, VAL ~47% pro forma; >$200M run-rate cost synergies; close 2H2026). No conventional spread for the acquirer, so deal_price = post-close re-rate for RIG = current $6.215 + RIG's per-combined-share slice of capitalized synergies (>$200M @ ~5x EV/EBITDA ~ $1.0B value; on ~2,250M combined sh ~ +$0.45) + modest scale/backlog re-rate ~$0.45 -> ~$7.10. undisturbed_price $6.00 = RIG pre-9-Feb vicinity (all-stock, so RIG itself carried little premium). break_prob 0.30: DOJ Second Request issued 4-May-2026 (RIG withdrew/refiled HSR 1-Apr/3-Apr) is a SUBSTANTIVE antitrust escalation on a two-largest-driller combination, but a global, fragmented market with many competitors makes outright block less likely than remedies/delay. HONEST CAVEAT: most of RIG's price variance here is oil-beta, not deal-spread; the floor is tight because the acquirer barely moved on announcement. Source: RIG/VAL 8-K & DEFA14A 9-Feb-2026; RIG 8-K DOJ Second Request 4-May-2026; RIG enterprise-values (1,109M sh, net debt ~$4.66B).
RIG.prob_basis: break_prob 0.30: DOJ Second Request (4-May-2026) signals genuine scrutiny of the #2/#3 floater combination; offset by a globally fragmented UDW/harsh-environment market and likely remedy/divestiture path rather than outright prohibition

RVTY.valuation_basis: CATALYST IS MIS-SPECIFIED/IMMATERIAL -> floor WIDENED per guardrail. The named event (China IDX sale, signed LOI 16-Apr-2026, up to $200M / ~$140M net consideration, $55M expected loss, definitive in Q2-2026 but close not until 2027) is ~1.2% of the $11.3B EV and DILUTIVE (~-$0.15 adj EPS). The real capital_return is the $1.0B buyback authorization (Oct-2025; ~$1.4B repurchased since 2023), ~9% of cap. announced_return_per_share $1.25 = net China proceeds $140M / 112M sh actually freed for return; residual_value $105 = standalone RVTY fair value at ~20x the $5.20-5.30 FY26 adj-EPS guide (ex-China), in line with de-rated life-science-tools peers ~20-22x. FV = 1.25 + 105 = $106.25 (stock ~fairly valued; this is NOT a high-conviction event). Floor $88 widened for tools-multiple compression + China/tariff overhang (52-wk low $81.2, 200dma $95). Source: RVTY Q1-2026 8-K/pro-forma (China IDX LOI up to $200M, $140M net, $55M loss, close 2027); RVTY 2026 guide $5.20-5.30 adj EPS ex-China; RVTY $1B buyback auth 27-Oct-2025.
RVTY.prob_basis: China IDX divestiture close is 'probable' per mgmt (2027) but immaterial and dilutive; capital-return value rests on the $1B buyback authorization, not the divestiture -> low event-edge, floor widened

STEL.valuation_basis: Merger-arb (target side). PB/STEL definitive merger (signed Jan 27, 2026): each STEL share receives $11.36 cash + 0.3803 PB shares (fixed ratio). Deal value at PB $69.63 = 11.36 + 0.3803*69.63 = $37.84/sh. ALL regulatory approvals obtained (FDIC, TX Dept of Banking, Fed Dallas waiver ~Apr 22, 2026); BOTH shareholder votes passed (STEL May 27, 2026). Only ~July 1, 2026 mechanical closing remains -> completion prob ~97%. Spot $37.695 already ~99.6% of deal value: residual spread is just time-value over ~3-4 weeks. Source: PB 424B3/S-4 + STEL DEFM14A (SEC, FY2026); businesswire/stocktitan Jan 28, 2026.
STEL.prob_basis: All regulatory approvals + both shareholder votes complete; only mechanical close pending. ~3% break/delay risk.

TALK.valuation_basis: Merger-arb (target side; triage mis-tagged to CDE/Coeur but the real UHS target is Talkspace). UHS to acquire TALK for $5.25/sh ALL-CASH, announced Mar 9, 2026 (EV ~$835M, funded off UHS revolver -> trivial financing risk for investment-grade UHS). Undisturbed pre-bid $4.76-$4.84 (Mar 6 close $4.76); jumped to $5.12 on Mar 9. Conditions: TALK shareholder vote + HSR/customary regulatory; expected Q3 2026 close, both boards unanimous. Deal_price $5.25; spot $5.21 = 99.2% of consideration, ~$0.04 gross over ~1 quarter. Completion prob ~95%. Source: UHS/TALK 8-K + PRNewswire Mar 9, 2026 (SEC FY2026).
TALK.prob_basis: All-cash, small EV easily financed by IG acquirer (UHS), unanimous boards, only TALK vote + HSR outstanding. ~5% break/delay risk.

UWMC.valuation_basis: WRONG-SIDE FLAG: UWMC is the ACQUIRER, not a target — there is NO bid for UWMC shares. The June 11 vote is a TWO (Two Harbors) shareholder vote on UWMC's contested $12.50 cash (or 2.3328 UWMC-share) offer vs CrossCountry's $12.00. Buying UWMC is NOT buying a deal target. If UWMC WINS, it pays ~107M TWO sh x $12.50 ~= $1.34B cash (vs UWMC $3.8B mcap) into a weak mortgage tape -> dilutive/leverage-negative for UWMC equity. Market confirms: UWMC fell $3.38 (May 8) -> $3.10 (May 13, on the raised bid) -> $2.515 now. As a LONG on this catalyst the skew is NEGATIVE. Fields below record UWMC's own standalone fair value, not the TWO deal price (deal_price field = the $12.50 UWMC PAYS, shown for reference only). Source: UWMC 8-K + DFAN14A May 11, 2026; housingwire/NMP.
UWMC.prob_basis: Contested bid (UWMC vs CrossCountry); outcome for TWO is ~coin-flip and, critically, winning is value-uncertain-to-negative for UWMC the acquirer. fair_value_target = UWMC standalone ~$2.50 (no positive re-rate from this catalyst; mild deal/dilution overhang); floor $2.00 = mortgage-cycle/rate-shock trough (52wk low $2.50 already近).

SUZ.valuation_basis: WRONG-SIDE FLAG: SUZ is the ACQUIRER of an asset, not a takeover target — there is NO bid for SUZ shares. Catalyst = closing the Kimberly-Clark international tissue JV: Suzano buys 51% for $1.734B cash (implies ~$3.4B EV/100%; 22 plants, 14 countries, ~1.0M t capacity), all competition approvals obtained (EU unconditional ~May 10; UK CMA cleared May 28, 2026), KMB-gated close in Q3 2026. Accretive but SMALL vs SUZ's pulp franchise ($10.0B mcap + heavy pulp net debt). SUZ weakness (spot $8.09 vs 200dma $9.43) is PULP-PRICE driven, not deal-driven, so the JV is not the swing factor. fair_value_target = modest post-close re-rate to ~$8.75 (tissue accretion + pulp stabilization at mid-cycle); floor $7.00 = pulp-trough / continued BHKP weakness. deal_price field = my fundamental target (no per-share bid exists for SUZ). Source: SUZ 6-K (SEC FY2026); emspreads/CNBC; CMA clearance May 28, 2026.
SUZ.prob_basis: JV close ~95% (all antitrust cleared, KMB-side corp mechanics only). But this is NOT a target-side arb — re-rate is a fundamental call on tissue accretion + pulp cycle, hence wide floor. reference_rr = (8.75-8.09)/(8.09-7.00)=0.66/1.09.

VALE.valuation_basis: Post-spin SoP once Vale Base Metals (VBM, nickel+copper) is IPO-separated. Iron Solutions FY25 adj EBITDA $13,803M @4.5x (low end of iron-ore majors Rio/BHP 4.5-5.0x; Vale discounted for Brazil/tailings risk) = $62,114M EV. VBM valued at $30,000M EV for 100% (between Manara's Apr-2024 validated $26B-EV mark and the bull ~$40B IPO estimate; credits VBM EBITDA +157% YoY in 4Q25 to ~$1.4B/qtr) x Vale's ~90% stake = $27,000M. Less net debt $13,746M (Q1-26), less Samarco/Brumadinho/decharacterization provisions $4,008M (Dec-25, off-net-debt) + iron-ops minority $900M = $4,908M adj. (89,114-13,746-4,908)/4,269 sh = $16.51. Build reconciles. Bull VBM-at-$40B case ~ +$2.1/sh ($18.6) noted but NOT used. Sources: Vale 4Q25 6-K (Iron Solutions $13.8B EBITDA), MINING.COM Mar 3 2026 (IPO mid-2026), Vale press release Apr-2024 (Manara 10% / $26B EV).

sop_components.basis: FY25 adj EBITDA $13,803M (Vale 4Q25 6-K). 4.5x = low end of iron-ore pure-plays Rio Tinto/BHP iron-ore 4.5-5.0x EV/EBITDA; Vale discounted for Brazil sovereign + Mariana/Samarco tailings overhang.

100% VBM EV $30,000M: anchored to Manara Apr-2024 deal implied $26B EV, uplifted for VBM EBITDA +157% YoY 4Q25 (~$1.4B/qtr on copper strength). Bull IPO estimate ~$40B (Procapitas/industry) held in reserve, not underwritten.

VECO.valuation_basis: Merger-arb, all-stock fixed ratio 0.3575 ACLS per VECO, NO collar (Agreement & Plan of Merger 30-Sep-2025; both shareholder votes PASSED 6-Feb-2026; only China SAMR clearance left; close H2-2026 — SEC 8-K/Form 425, gurufocus 6-Feb-2026). Deal value = 0.3575 x ACLS $157.00 = $56.13/sh. CRITICAL: VECO trades $61.74, ~$5.6 ABOVE deal value (both names ripped today, ACLS +6.7%/VECO +5.6% on SAMR/sector optimism) => NEGATIVE arb spread; riding to close at the current ratio LOSES ~9%. The arb catalyst has largely fired; remaining edge is only if ACLS re-rates higher or terms are sweetened.

VG.valuation_basis: Litigation-overhang 'recovery' (NOT bankruptcy). Counterparty arbitrations: Repsol (Jan-2026 ICC) and Shell (Aug-2025 ICC) resolved FOR VG; Edison settled Mar-2026; BP went ADVERSE — VG found liable under post-COD SPA, BP seeking $3.7bn to $6bn+, quantum hearing pending 2026/2027 (Reuters/LNG Prime; SEC 8-K 21-Jan-2026). Clean (pre-BP) sell-side equity clusters $16-18 (JPMorgan OW $17, consensus ~$16.25-17.12 — MarketBeat/Investing.com Jun-2026). Recovery_value = clean ~$17 LESS realistic BP drag ~$2/sh (mid ~$4.5bn award / 2.442bn sh, after-tax) ≈ $15. Net debt $35.7bn, PP&E $50.5bn (LNG terminals), minority $3.48bn, pref $1.63bn; 2.442bn shares (multi-class). Stock $12.92 already discounts most of the BP overhang.

VSCO.valuation_basis: Activist catalyst (BBRC 13%, vote AGAINST Chair Donna James at Jun-11-2026 AGM) carries NO price target, NO sale/take-private, NO breakup, NO SoP (BBRC DFAN14A) and all three proxy advisors (ISS, Glass Lewis, Egan-Jones) back the incumbent board -> a governance protest with no value-unlock event. Stock +141% off lows and +50% over the 50d avg ($50.33) on a Q1 EARNINGS beat (swung to $47.7M profit, raised FY guide), NOT the activist. My own SoP: VS&PINK core ~$560M EBITDA @8.0x (ANF turnaround comp ~7-8x; brand premium) = $4,480M; Adore Me/digital ~$90M @9.0x (DTC) = $810M; sum EV $5,290M less net debt $2,644M (Q1-FY26, May-26) less minority $55M = $2,591M /83.4 sh = $31.07. At $76.22 the stock trades ~14x fwd EBITDA vs apparel peers 5-7x -> NO_UPSIDE; the activist adds nothing. Sources: BBRC DFAN14A, VSCO Q1-FY26 10-Q (net debt), stocktitan Jun-2026 (3 proxy advisors FOR board).

sop_components.segment: Victoria's Secret & PINK core (NA + Intl stores/digital)
sop_components.basis: Fwd EBITDA ~$560M (FY25 EBIT $297M + D&A ~$253M, credit raised FY guide). 8.0x = premium to specialty-apparel peers (Gap/AEO 5-6x); benchmarked to Abercrombie & Fitch ANF ~7-8x at turnaround peak, for brand value + momentum.
~$90M EBITDA @9.0x; digital-native DTC commands a premium to store-based apparel (~9x).

WHR.valuation_basis: Forced-seller = monetizing the Whirlpool-of-India stake to delever. The Nov-2025 leg (51%->39.76%, deconsolidation, ~$166M + $251M gain) already fired; the dead Advent '$550-600M/31%' plan collapsed Dec-2025 on valuation, but a LIVE auction continues (EQT/Bain lead bidders, binding offers ~Aug, WHR retaining ~20%). Proceeds go to debt paydown (NAV-neutral). SoP: core appliances (ex-India) on a NORMALIZED EBITDA ~$1,300M (partial recovery off the 4% trough margin toward 9% mid-cycle; FY26 guide is trough: ~$15.0B sales x ~4% EBIT + D&A) @5.5x (Electrolux/appliance peers 5-6x) = $7,150M; residual ~40% listed India stake at market ($1,375M full India equity x 0.40) = $550M; sum EV $7,700M less net debt $6,478M (Q1-26) less pension/OPEB underfunding ~$300M = $922M /64.8 sh = $14.23. At $39.56 the market prices a FULL mid-cycle (9%-margin) recovery on a thin levered equity sliver (net debt $6.5B vs $2.6B cap, EV ~9.6x fwd EBITDA) -> NO_UPSIDE. Sources: MINING.COM/SEC 8-K (Nov-2025 sell-down), Business Standard Jul-2026 (EQT/Bain), WHR Q1-26 slides + FY26 guide (4% EBIT, FCF >$300M, net-debt <$5B target), Whirlpool-of-India NSE mkt cap.

sop_components.segment: Core appliances ex-India (MDA NA + EMEA + LatAm)
sop_components.basis: Normalized EBITDA ~$1,300M (~6.5% EBIT margin, a PARTIAL recovery off the FY26 4%-margin trough toward the 9% mid-cycle target; not peak). 5.5x = appliance peers Electrolux ~5x, Helen of Troy 5-6x EV/EBITDA.

sop_components.segment: Whirlpool of India residual stake (~40%, NSE-listed)
sop_components.basis: Full Whirlpool-of-India equity ~$1,375M (~INR 11,000 cr, NSE May-2026) x WHR's 39.76% = ~$550M market mark. EQT/Bain auction validates ~$550-600M for the larger 31% block.

XENE.valuation_basis: Barbell — the asymmetric Ph3 readout is BEHIND us and captured. X-TOLE2 (focal-onset seizures) HIT positive 9-Mar-2026 (-53.2%/-34.5% median FOS reduction at 25mg/15mg vs -10.4% placebo); stock spiked to $63.95, faded to $51.27 (GlobeNewswire 9-Mar; NeurologyLive). Remaining binary = FDA approval + launch: NDA Q3-2026, market late-2027/early-2028. win_prob 0.85 (de-risked: TWO positive pivotals X-TOLE + X-TOLE2, clean safety, KV7 mechanism). target_on_win $74 = sell-side Buy target on peak sales $2bn+ ('conservative' per Jefferies; +bipolar/MDD optionality). downside_on_loss $18 ~ net cash $1.33bn / ~79M sh = ~$17 floor on a CRL. Prob-weighted EV = 0.85x$74 + 0.15x$18 = $65.6 vs $51.27 spot => +28% expected (EV%).
XENE.prob_basis: Approval probability given TWO positive Phase 3 pivotals (X-TOLE + X-TOLE2) in FOS, clean safety, established KV7 potassium-channel mechanism; NDA filing Q3-2026

ZD.valuation_basis: Value-surfacing catalyst (activist Pale Fire Capital 6.9% on capitalization/board; no public PT) = the definitive $1.2B-cash sale of Connectivity (Ookla/Speedtest/Ekahau/Downdetector/RootMetrics) to Accenture, announced Mar 3 2026, closing 'in coming months'. Post-close SoP: RemainCo adj EBITDA ~$261M (FY25 total adj EBITDA $381.4M less Connectivity ~$120M at its >50% margin on $231M rev) @6.0x (ZD blended currently ~5.4x; modest re-rate for the cleaner, buyback-fueled story, discounted for AI-search/secular decline at PCMag/Mashable/IGN) = $1,566M EV. Pro-forma the balance sheet to NET CASH: current net debt $347M (Q1-26) less ~$950M net Accenture proceeds ($1.2B less ~$250M tax on low-basis assets) = -$603M net cash. (1,566 - (-603))/36.84 sh = $58.88. Genuine residual upside vs $46.60: market still values the whole co ~5.4x and under-credits the net-cash-rich RemainCo re-rate. 25M-sh buyback authorization ($51.6M used Q1-26) supports. Sources: ZD 8-K Mar-3-2026 (Accenture $1.2B), ZD 4Q25 release (adj EBITDA $381.4M), Q1-25 slides (Connectivity >50% EBITDA margin), ZD 10-Q (36.84M sh, net debt).

sop_components.segment: RemainCo (Tech & Shopping, Health & Wellness, Cybersecurity & Martech, Gaming) post-Connectivity
sop_components.basis: RemainCo adj EBITDA ~$261M = FY25 total $381.4M less Connectivity ~$120M (>50% margin on $231M rev). 6.0x = modest premium to ZD's current ~5.4x blended; digital-media conglomerate comp IAC 6-8x, discounted for AI-search/traffic secular decline.
